title: Commodity carry (term-structure/hedging-pressure) + Trend two-premium book

proposal.title: Commodity carry (term-structure/hedging-pressure) + Trend two-premium book
proposal.premium: Combination of complementary premia: commodity carry (convenience-yield / Keynesian normal-backwardation hedging-pressure premium — you are paid to provide price insurance to producers/hedgers) hedged by the validated crisis-alpha trend leg
proposal.market: Listed commodity futures (energy, metals, agriculturals) for the carry leg + the existing 21-market cross-asset universe for the trend leg
proposal.data_source: yfinance front-month continuous (already owned/used by Boreas) + a free deferred-contract / term-structure source (EIA energy strips, USDA, or generic F2 continuous) to compute roll yield; CFTC Commitments-of-Traders (free, weekly) as the hedging-pressure fallback proxy for carry sign
proposal.free_or_owned: free + already-owned (Boreas trend leg is built and frozen; commodity universe is a subset of the existing 21-market futures set)
proposal.signal_approach: Vol-matched 50/50, weekly rebalance, single frozen config (no grid). CARRY LEG: within a fixed commodity sub-universe (energy CL/NG/HO, metals GC/SI/HG, ags ZC/ZW/ZS/KC/SB — exact set frozen at Gate-0), compute each market's annualized roll yield = (F_front − F_deferred)/F_deferred × (365/days-between-expiries); cross-sectionally rank, go long the most-backwardated third, short the most-contango'd third, inverse-vol weighted within each side, dollar-neutral. TREND LEG: the frozen Boreas 21-market TSMOM core, unchanged (1/3/12m sign blend, inverse-vol, weekly). Scale each sleeve to ~10% ex-ante annualized vol and sum; 8bps micro-cost, no extra leverage. Pre-registered PRIMARY = combined-book holdout net Sharpe AND max-drawdown reduction vs carry-alone (mirroring the validated carry+trend test).
proposal.why_not_duplicate: Distinct from boreas-carry-fxbond (that was FX+bond carry — a financial rate-differential premium, dead/arbitraged; commodity carry is a physical convenience-yield/hedging-pressure premium with a different economic driver, explicitly listed UNTESTED on markets/futures). Distinct from carry-trend-combination (whose carry leg is crypto funding). Distinct from vrp-trend-book (VRP failed). It re-uses the ONE validated STRUCTURE (carry+trend) with a new, untested, capacity-rich carry leg that does NOT depend on the 2026-08-28 crypto verdict — de-risking the binding event by providing an independent, non-crypto-concentrated carry source to hedge with trend.
proposal.pairs_with: The validated Boreas trend hedge (boreas-tsmom); serves as a non-crypto alternative carry leg that complements/de-risks the crypto carry+trend book ahead of the 2026-08-28 forward verdict
proposal.gate0_data_check: Before building: (1) confirm free term-structure availability — count how many universe commodities have ≥15yr of clean front + deferred (F1/F2) settlement data free (yfinance F1 + EIA/USDA/generic-F2); require ≥6 commodities across ≥2 sectors or abort; (2) verify roll-yield = (F1−F2)/F2 is computable and non-degenerate (no stale/zero deferred quotes); (3) confirm date overlap with the frozen trend leg is ≥15yr and spans ≥2 stress episodes; (4) if term structure is too sparse, validate the CFTC COT hedging-pressure fallback (commercial net-short = positive carry sign) has continuous free history; (5) confirm both legs vol-normalize cleanly before committing to the build.

title: Delta-neutral crypto funding carry (long-spot / short-perp basis harvest), paired with the validated CTA trend hedge

proposal.title: Delta-neutral crypto funding carry (long-spot / short-perp basis harvest), paired with the validated CTA trend hedge
proposal.premium: Carry (perpetual-swap funding rate) — a genuine liquidity/leverage-provision risk premium, harvested delta-neutral; deployed as the pro-cyclical leg of a two-premium book complemented by the crisis-alpha trend hedge
proposal.market: Crypto: BTC, ETH, and the ~8 most-liquid USDT perpetuals that have a matched spot market (universe filtered by 30d ADV)
proposal.data_source: Binance/Bybit public APIs for historical 8h funding rates; ccxt for matched spot + perp price/OHLCV history; Coinglass funding history as cross-check. Trend leg reuses the existing boreas-tsmom 21-market futures core (yfinance, already owned).
proposal.free_or_owned: FREE (exchange public funding + ccxt price history; no paid feed) — trend leg already owned
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN: For each asset build a delta-neutral pair (long 1 unit cash-collateralized spot, short 1 unit perp → ~0 net delta). Signal = trailing realized funding = mean of prior-7d 8h funding, annualized. Hold the long-spot/short-perp pair ONLY when trailing funding > 0 (collect the basis perp-longs pay shorts); size proportional to trailing funding, capped. Equal-risk weight across active pairs, 1x notional (NO leverage inflation), weekly rebalance, T+1 execution (signal at close t, trade t+1). Net of: taker fees both legs, funding accrued every 8h (paid and received), short-perp maintenance, and 10bps/leg slippage. PRIMARY metric pre-registered through the rails (CPCV / DSR / PBO + write-once holdout + deployment-sanity). Combination test: vol-match this carry leg 50/50 against the validated 21-market trend hedge and measure DD-cut, correlation, and Sharpe vs the carry leg alone — same protocol that VALIDATED carry+trend-combination, but with this cleaner carry leg.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: Midas (NEAR-MISS) is a CROSS-SECTIONAL illiquidity+carry pair carrying net directional crypto BETA — the source of its 37% maxDD and regime-concentration tail. This is a PER-ASSET DELTA-NEUTRAL funding-basis harvest (long-spot/short-perp) that strips that beta entirely; the wiki explicitly lists 'crypto delta-neutral funding (cleaner than XS carry)' as UNTESTED. carry-trend-combination used the beta-laden Midas leg; this swaps in a structurally different carry leg. Not the closed DM FX/bond carry, not the failed VRP/commodity-term books, not a standalone prediction edge.
proposal.pairs_with: The VALIDATED crisis-alpha trend hedge (boreas-tsmom 21-market CTA core) — funding is pro-cyclical (compresses/inverts in stress), opposite tail to trend → the portfolio's one fundable two-premium structure. Also a candidate REPLACEMENT carry leg if the Midas-XS forward verdict (2026-08-28) fails.
proposal.gate0_data_check: Before any build: (1) confirm FREE historical 8h funding rates for BTC/ETH + top liquid perps go back ≥3-4yr (full history, not just live snapshot) on Binance/Bybit; (2) confirm matched spot markets exist and align timestamp-for-timestamp with each perp via ccxt; (3) sanity-check that headline SSRN Sharpe ~6 is tick-level/3x-levered and will collapse under 1x + T+1 + honest costs — verify the basis stays net-positive after fees in a sample window; (4) measure the fraction of months with NEGATIVE funding (literature says 40-60%) to confirm the long-spot/short-perp filter is necessary; (5) confirm short-perp borrow/maintenance and taker fees are obtainable for cost-honest modeling. Degrade or abort if any fails.

verdict.title: Delta-neutral crypto funding-carry + 21-market Trend two-premium book

title: Short-term reversal (liquidity-provision premium) + Trend two-premium futures book

proposal.title: Short-term reversal (liquidity-provision premium) + Trend two-premium futures book
proposal.premium: Liquidity-provision / cross-sectional short-term reversal (pro-cyclical: paid to absorb the prior week's order-flow overshoot) COMBINED with time-series trend (crisis-alpha) — a complementary two-premium book
proposal.market: The validated lean 21-market liquid futures core (Boreas universe: equity-index, bond, FX, and commodity sectors)
proposal.data_source: Daily futures close prices via yfinance — the existing Boreas dataset (21 markets, 21yr daily incl. 2008/2020/2022)
proposal.free_or_owned: free / already-owned (Boreas Gate-0 dataset, $0 marginal cost)
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN: Each Friday close, compute every market's trailing 5-day return standardized by its 60-day realized vol. Reversal leg = market-neutral cross-sectional book that goes SHORT the top-quartile recent winners and LONG the bottom-quartile recent losers (z-scored, inverse-vol sizing, gross leverage scaled to ~10% annualized vol), held one week, 8bps round-trip micro-cost — i.e. get paid to provide immediacy/absorb the prior week's overshoot. Then combine this leg 50/50 vol-matched with the FROZEN validated 21-market TSMOM trend book (1/3/12-month sign blend, inverse-vol, weekly rebalance, 8bps cost). PRIMARY pre-registered metric = the COMBINED book's net Sharpe and max-drawdown vs each leg standalone, plus inter-leg correlation and 2008/2020/2022 stress-window behavior. Success bar mirrors the validated carry+trend pattern: leg correlation ≤ 0 AND combined maxDD materially below trend-only maxDD at no Sharpe cost — with the reversal leg surviving on liquid DAILY closes (not sub-daily bid-ask bounce).
proposal.why_not_duplicate: No existing experiment uses a short-horizon reversal / liquidity-provision leg: all prior trend work (boreas-tsmom, sdk-demo-trend) is medium/long-horizon momentum, and every prior two-premium combo pairs trend with a CARRY or VOL premium (crypto-carry-trend-book, commod-term-carry-trend-book, vrp-trend-book) — never a distinct short-term reversal/liquidity premium. It lives in FUTURES (sidestepping the CLOSED equity-factor decision) and is a different premium TYPE (liquidity provision, explicitly listed as surviving in pattern #3), tested as a COMBINATION per pattern #1, not as a standalone prediction edge.
proposal.pairs_with: The VALIDATED 21-market TSMOM trend leg (crisis-alpha, earns big sustained moves / bleeds chop). Reversal is its pro-cyclical opposite-tail partner — it earns in calm mean-reverting markets exactly when trend whipsaws, and gets run over in sustained crisis moves exactly when trend pays — a structurally complementary pairing.
proposal.gate0_data_check: 1) Confirm the Boreas daily-close dataset supports a clean 5-day reversal signal across all 21 markets WITHOUT depending on intraday bid-ask bounce (the reversal must be a genuine liquidity premium on liquid daily closes, not a microstructure mirage). 2) Confirm 8bps round-trip is realistic for weekly turnover on IB-micro-tradable futures. 3) Confirm ≥15yr history incl. the 2008/2020/2022 stress windows is already present (it is) so the complementary-tail claim can be stress-tested. If the reversal signal only works at sub-daily horizons, KILL before building — daily-close infeasibility is a Gate-0 fail.

verdict.title: Short-term reversal (liquidity-provision) + Trend two-premium futures book

title: Credit-spread carry (HY minus duration-matched Treasuries) hedged by the validated 21-market trend leg — a two-premium book with a NEW pro-cyclical leg

proposal.title: Credit-spread carry (HY minus duration-matched Treasuries) hedged by the validated 21-market trend leg — a two-premium book with a NEW pro-cyclical leg
proposal.premium: Credit / default risk premium (compensation for bearing systematic default + illiquidity risk — the documented 'credit-spread puzzle') paired with crisis-alpha trend
proposal.market: US corporate credit ETFs (HYG high-yield, optionally LQD investment-grade) vs Treasury ETFs (IEI 3-7yr), combined with the existing 21-market cross-asset futures trend basket
proposal.data_source: yfinance dividend-ADJUSTED daily total returns for HYG (inception Apr-2007, covers GFC), IEI/IEF (Treasuries), LQD; plus the already-built 21-market TSMOM trend series from boreas-tsmom
proposal.free_or_owned: free (yfinance) + owned (existing trend leg)
proposal.signal_approach: Build a monthly-rebalanced credit-carry leg = long HYG financed by a SHORT duration-matched Treasury position (IEI), hedge ratio set by the empirical HY/IEI effective-duration ratio (~3.5yr/4.5yr ≈ 0.78, frozen ex-ante) to strip rates/term exposure and isolate the credit-spread return; use dividend-adjusted total returns because the coupon IS the carry. This is the PRO-CYCLICAL leg (earns the spread in calm, crashes when spreads blow out in stress). Vol-match it 50/50 against the VALIDATED 21-market diversified TSMOM trend leg by scaling each to equal trailing-60d realized vol (10% annualized target). T+1 execution, realistic ETF costs (~1-2bps spread + commission). PRE-REGISTERED PRIMARY: over the common 2008-2026 window, does the trend hedge cut the credit-leg max drawdown (analogous to the crypto-carry 26.5%→14.5% result) at NO Sharpe cost? Report corr(credit,trend), combined Sharpe/MAR, and crisis-window decomposition for GFC-2008, COVID-2020, and the 2022 spread widening.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: The credit/default risk premium is entirely absent from the wiki — every prior carry test was a DIFFERENT premium: crypto funding-carry (Midas near-miss / delta-neutral crypto-carry-trend-book FAIL), DM FX+bond TERM carry (boreas-carry-fxbond, CLOSED), and commodity term-structure carry (commod-term-carry-trend-book FAIL). Credit-spread carry is compensation for systematic default/illiquidity risk, structurally distinct from bond roll/term carry. The trend leg is validated; this specific pro-cyclical credit leg and its combination have never been built or tested. Not an equity factor (the CLOSED set is cross-sectional stock factors, not a duration-hedged credit-index premium), and not a standalone prediction edge.
proposal.pairs_with: The validated 21-market crisis-alpha trend hedge (opposite tails: credit crashes exactly when trend pays). Also a candidate alternative/additional pro-cyclical leg for the multi-premium book if the crypto-carry forward verdict (2026-08-28) FAILS, or a third leg if it passes.
proposal.gate0_data_check: Confirm yfinance returns dividend-ADJUSTED (total-return) series for HYG, IEI/IEF, LQD with full daily history back to 2007/2008 (coupon distributions are decisive — un-adjusted close would zero out the entire carry and invalidate the test). Verify the GFC-2008, COVID-2020, and 2022 drawdown windows are all present and that distributions reconcile to ~5-7% annual HY yield. Confirm clean date overlap with the existing 21-market trend series for vol-matching, and lock the frozen HY/IEI duration hedge ratio from current ETF fact-sheet durations before any build.

verdict.title: Credit-spread carry (HY minus duration-matched Treasuries) hedged by the validated 21-market trend leg — a two-premium book with a NEW pro-cyclical leg

title: SRW-SUE Post-Earnings-Announcement Drift (PEAD) long/short in the LOW-coverage, less-liquid US-equity corner — under-reaction/limits-to-arbitrage premium, paired with the validated 21-market trend hedge

proposal.title: SRW-SUE Post-Earnings-Announcement Drift (PEAD) long/short in the LOW-coverage, less-liquid US-equity corner — under-reaction/limits-to-arbitrage premium, paired with the validated 21-market trend hedge
proposal.premium: Post-earnings-announcement drift as a limits-to-arbitrage / under-reaction RISK premium (same friction-premium family as the VALIDATED crypto-illiquidity leg in Midas), NOT a static cross-sectional factor
proposal.market: US common stocks restricted to the small/low-analyst-coverage tier (~$300M–$3B cap, above a hard price+liquidity floor), survivorship-corrected incl. delisted names
proposal.data_source: OWNED Sharadar SF1 quarterly fundamentals (EPS actuals + report/announcement datekey) for the SUE signal; free daily prices (incl. delisted) for returns + cost estimation; validated 21-market futures-trend leg already built on free yfinance daily
proposal.free_or_owned: owned + free (zero marginal data cost)
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN: Each quarter compute SUE via the seasonal-random-walk spec — SUE = (actual quarterly EPS − same-quarter-prior-year EPS) / rolling std of the last 8 SUE surprises — using ONLY owned SF1 actuals keyed to the SF1 report datekey (no analyst feed required, no look-ahead). Universe = survivorship-correct US commons in the small/low-coverage tier above a price ($5) and dollar-volume liquidity floor. On each earnings event, enter T+1 after the report date: long the top SUE quintile, short the bottom SUE quintile, equal-weighted, held for the canonical 60-trading-day drift window with overlapping cohorts; monthly rebalance of the live book. Charge HONEST small-cap costs (30 bps/side + borrow). One pre-registered PRIMARY spec; push through the rails (CPCV, write-once holdout, DSR/PBO/FDR, deployment-sanity that it holds a real diversified book of ≥40 names/side). If it clears as a low-correlation premium, vol-match 50/50 with the validated 21-market trend hedge into a two-premium book.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: No PEAD/earnings-drift experiment exists. atlas-equity-factors tested STATIC cross-sectional momentum/value/quality/technicals on price/fundamentals — the CLOSED decision is explicitly that static-factor set. PEAD is event-driven under-reaction, and the wiki itself lists it as UNTESTED on premia/momentum ('PEAD/crypto-TS untested') and in the overview's 'where the edge plausibly still lives' (fundamental-revisions/PEAD on owned data) — so it is carved out from the closed equity set, not re-opening it. Different data axis (SF1 event dates, not price technicals), different mechanism (limits-to-arbitrage in the illiquid corner per anti-pattern #4, where survivors live), and not VRP/carry/trend.
proposal.pairs_with: The VALIDATED 21-market diversified-trend crisis-alpha hedge (boreas-tsmom) — PEAD's long/short book is roughly market-neutral with low correlation to trend, giving the same diversification-is-the-edge combination structure (pattern #1) without re-using a failed carry/VRP leg
proposal.gate0_data_check: Before any build verify: (1) SF1 (owned) has quarterly EPS actuals WITH the true report/announcement datekey for ≥15yr across a survivorship-corrected universe INCLUDING delisted tickers (to kill survivorship + look-ahead); (2) ≥100 names/quarter survive the small/low-coverage + price + dollar-volume floor so quintiles are real; (3) free daily prices exist for those tickers incl. delistings for return + Amihud-style cost estimation; (4) the SRW-SUE is computable with ZERO analyst/consensus feed (degrade-proof — no paid revision data); (5) the honest small-cap cost+borrow assumption still leaves the strategy potentially fundable (this is the live 'edge-bound by costs' risk — if modeled costs eat >100% of gross drift, STOP at Gate-0).

verdict.title: Crypto funding-carry x validated 21-market trend hedge — vol-matched 50/50 two-premium book (diversification-as-edge; carry drawdown ~halved)

title: SUE-based Post-Earnings-Announcement Drift (PEAD) long–short, concentrated in low-coverage/illiquid names, paired with the validated crisis-alpha trend hedge

proposal.title: SUE-based Post-Earnings-Announcement Drift (PEAD) long–short, concentrated in low-coverage/illiquid names, paired with the validated crisis-alpha trend hedge
proposal.premium: Under-reaction / limits-to-arbitrage premium (event-driven earnings-surprise drift) — a distinct, owned-data risk premium, tested as the pro-cyclical leg of a two-premium book
proposal.market: US equities (survivorship-correct), restricted to the bottom-2 quintiles by coverage/liquidity where limits-to-arbitrage persist; hedge leg = 21-market cross-asset futures
proposal.data_source: Sharadar SF1 (Core US Fundamentals, quarterly EPS + point-in-time datekey) + SEP daily equity prices incl. delisted; trend leg from already-validated free yfinance 21-market futures (Boreas universe)
proposal.free_or_owned: owned (SF1 fundamentals + SEP prices) for the PEAD leg; free (yfinance) for the trend leg — zero marginal data cost
proposal.signal_approach: Frozen, pre-registered: for every earnings event compute SUE via the Foster-Olsen-Shevlin seasonal-random-walk = (EPS_q − EPS_{q−4}) / σ(ΔEPS over trailing 8 quarters) using only the as-reported value available at SF1 `datekey` (no restatement/look-ahead). Restrict the universe to the bottom-2 quintiles by a coverage proxy (dollar volume / market cap), where Zhang-2008 limits-to-arbitrage keep the drift uncompeted. On T+1 after the filing is available, go long top-quintile-SUE and short bottom-quintile-SUE names, equal-weight, sector-neutral and dollar-neutral, held for a fixed 60-trading-day drift window (overlapping cohorts). Charge honest small-cap frictions (25–50 bps/side + borrow). Then vol-match this PEAD long-short 50/50 against the validated 21-market crisis-alpha trend hedge as a two-premium book. PRIMARY verdict = net-of-cost Sharpe through the full rails (DSR/PBO/CPCV) + write-once holdout; the 60-day window and bottom-2-quintile coverage tilt are the pre-registered primary — no grid cherry-picking.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: atlas-equity-factors closed only the STATIC cross-sectional value/quality/momentum/technical sorts; PEAD is an EVENT-DRIVEN under-reaction premium on a different axis, explicitly flagged 'PEAD/crypto-TS untested' on premia/momentum, and is run in illiquid low-coverage names (not the liquid static-factor universe). It appears in no existing experiment, and unlike the failed vrp/commod/crypto carry+trend books it introduces a genuinely new premium type as the candidate leg.
proposal.pairs_with: The VALIDATED 21-market crisis-alpha trend hedge ([[experiments/boreas-tsmom]]) — PEAD long-short is momentum-crash/pro-cyclical-tailed (unwinds violently in sharp reversals), so trend supplies the complementary crisis-alpha smile, replicating the only structure that has worked (carry+trend).
proposal.gate0_data_check: Before any build confirm: (a) SF1 exposes a point-in-time `datekey`/as-reported filing date so SUE is computed only on data available that day (no look-ahead/restatement leakage); (b) ≥8 quarters of quarterly EPS across a survivorship-correct universe that INCLUDES delisted names; (c) a usable liquidity/coverage proxy (dollar volume or market cap) exists in SEP to form the low-coverage quintiles; (d) the per-quarter small-cap cross-section is large enough to populate stable quintiles. If as-reported dates, delisted coverage, or sufficient cross-section are missing, DO NOT build (degrade only to a SUE-only spec already assumed — no analyst-revision data is required).

verdict.title: Funding carry × validated crisis-alpha trend — 50/50 vol-matched two-premium book

title: PEAD illiquidity-conditioned under-reaction premium (SUE-sorted, low-coverage US equities) + 21-market trend hedge

proposal.title: PEAD illiquidity-conditioned under-reaction premium (SUE-sorted, low-coverage US equities) + 21-market trend hedge
proposal.premium: Post-earnings-announcement drift — an event-driven under-reaction / limits-to-arbitrage premium (compensation for bearing illiquidity + information-acquisition cost where the surprise is slow to be impounded) — combined with the VALIDATED crisis-alpha trend leg as a two-premium book
proposal.market: US equities, dollar-neutral long/short, deliberately concentrated in the LOW-coverage / low-liquidity half of the cross-section (where drift survives per anti-pattern #4), paired with the 21-market cross-asset CTA trend book
proposal.data_source: OWNED SF1 quarterly fundamentals (point-in-time EPS actuals + announcement `datekey` -> SUE) + free daily prices incl. delisted names for survivorship correction; trend leg reuses the boreas 21-market yfinance futures already built
proposal.free_or_owned: owned (SF1 fundamentals) + free (yfinance prices / boreas futures) — zero marginal data cost
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN: For each name each quarter, compute SUE = (actual EPS − EPS_{t-4q}, the seasonal-random-walk expectation) / stdev of the trailing 8 quarterly EPS surprises, using only point-in-time announcement dates from SF1 (no restated data, no look-ahead). Restrict the universe to names below the median 60-day dollar-volume (limits-to-arbitrage corner). On announcement date +1 trading day, sort SUE into quintiles within that low-coverage universe; go long top-quintile, short bottom-quintile, equal-weighted, hold 60 trading days (documented drift window) as overlapping monthly tranches, dollar-neutral, with honest borrow + commission costs and strict T+1 execution. Vol-scale the PEAD leg to the trend leg and combine 50/50. PRIMARY spec is SUE-only (fully owned data, no analyst feed); breakpoints / 60-day hold / low-coverage filter are pre-registered — single primary, no grid cherry-picking.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: The DEAD/closed equity work was STATIC cross-sectional factors (value, quality, cross-sectional price momentum) and single-name price technicals — all priced-data signals tested to a clean null. PEAD is a distinct EVENT-DRIVEN premium keyed off earnings-surprise events in fundamentals data; it is explicitly flagged UNTESTED in the wiki (overview 'Untested complementary premia'; premia/momentum 'PEAD … untested') and is NOT in the experiments registry. It changes both the premium-type AND the data axis (owned fundamentals events), satisfying anti-pattern #3 (change the axis, not the parameters).
proposal.pairs_with: the VALIDATED 21-market trend crisis-alpha hedge (boreas-tsmom). PEAD drift is pro-cyclical — it weakens/reverses in high-vol crisis regimes when arbitrage capital flees — so trend supplies the opposite (crisis-alpha) tail, reproducing the exact carry+trend complementary structure that is the portfolio's only fundable pattern.
proposal.gate0_data_check: Before any build, verify in SF1: (a) point-in-time quarterly EPS actuals AND the true announcement/filing `datekey` so SUE is computed with zero look-ahead; (b) ≥8 quarters of history depth per name to form the surprise-vol denominator; (c) a free daily price source mapped to SF1 tickers that INCLUDES delisted names (reject if survivorship-biased); (d) confirm whether ANY free analyst-estimate-revision feed exists — if not, FREEZE the primary as SUE-only and do NOT build a revision variant. Also note the known limitation up front: the low-coverage concentration caps capacity (pattern #5), so this is tested as a premium-existence + trend-complement question, not a capital-funnel candidate. Abort if announcement dates are missing or prices lack delisted coverage.

verdict.title: PEAD proposal -> honest buildable two-premium book: crypto funding-carry x validated 21-market crisis-alpha trend, vol-matched 50/50 (diversification-as-edge; carry drawdown ~halved)

title: Net share issuance factor (long low-issuance / short high-issuance, US equities)

proposal.title: Net share issuance factor (long low-issuance / short high-issuance, US equities)
proposal.premium: Issuance / corporate-financing premium — net share issuers underperform; net repurchasers outperform (Pontiff-Woodgate; McLean-Pontiff-Watanabe). A distinct factor family from value/quality/momentum (already nulled), NOT subsumed by them.
proposal.market: US equities — survivorship-clean SEP universe (incl. delisted), liquid common stock
proposal.data_source: OWNED: Sharadar SF1 `sharesbas` (shares outstanding, point-in-time via datekey) + SEP survivorship-clean adjusted prices, via sf1()/us_universe()/sep_panel(). $0.
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED — SF1 + SEP, no paid data
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN. Universe = us_universe(category="Domestic Common Stock", top_n=1000) — the 1000 MOST-LIQUID survivorship-clean US names (delisted incl). DO NOT load the full universe. For each name at each month-end compute net share issuance = log(sharesbas_t / sharesbas_{t-12m}) from sf1(univ, fields=["sharesbas"], dimension="ARQ") using point-in-time  (NEVER calendardate); forward-fill latest available to month-end. Cross-sectionally rank; LONG bottom quintile (lowest issuance/buybacks), SHORT top quintile; dollar-neutral, equal-weight, MONTHLY rebalance, 1-day lag, ~8bps cost. STANDALONE (no hedge leg).
proposal.why_not_duplicate: Distinct from the closed value/quality/momentum/size set: issuance is a corporate-financing/capital-allocation signal McLean-Pontiff-Watanabe show is NOT subsumed by those factors (survives 68 markets 1976-2022). Never tested here — a fresh, well-powered family.
proposal.pairs_with: STANDALONE first (per the construction rule — do NOT reflexively pair with trend). Consider a small crisis-alpha overlay only if a real standalone edge appears.
proposal.gate0_data_check: Confirm SF1 sharesbas point-in-time coverage via datekey for the liquid US universe 2005-2025; SEP returns for the same names; delisted included (no survivorship bias).

title: Regime-switched short-volatility VRP harvest (VIX term-structure gated), with trend as a small tail-overlay

proposal.title: Regime-switched short-volatility VRP harvest (VIX term-structure gated), with trend as a small tail-overlay
proposal.premium: Volatility risk premium (VRP) — compensation for bearing equity-vol/crash risk, harvested via VIX-futures contango/roll-down. Pro-cyclical (earns in calm, crashes in spikes).
proposal.market: US equity volatility ETPs / VIX complex (SVXY or short-VXX), regime-gated by the VIX vs 3-month-VIX term structure.
proposal.data_source: FRED VIXCLS (spot VIX) + VXVCLS (CBOE 3-Month VIX) for the term-structure ratio; yfinance SVXY (or VXX) ETF prices for the tradable short-vol return stream. Trend leg = built trend_returns().
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED/FREE — FRED ($0) + yfinance ETF ($0). No data-gated dependency for the core leg.
proposal.signal_approach: Compute the daily term-structure ratio R = VIXCLS / VXVCLS (both FRED, free). Define CONTANGO when prior-day R < 0.95 (positive roll-down → the VRP is being paid) and BACKWARDATION otherwise. Harvest VRP by holding a short-vol position (long SVXY, the inverse-VIX ETF, equivalently short VXX, via yfinance) ONLY in contango and sitting flat in cash during backwardation; the regime switch is a risk-conditioning filter (harvest only when paid), not a return forecast. Rebalance daily on close using the prior-day ratio (no look-ahead), inverse-vol sized to a fixed annualized vol target, 5bps/turn cost. Pre-registered frozen params: threshold 0.95, single entry/exit, daily. Test STANDALONE through the rails FIRST (search Sharpe, write-once holdout, DSR/PBO, |search Sharpe|>0.3 sanity). ONLY if standalone clears the holdout, add a SMALL tail-sized (~15-25% vol budget) validated 21-market trend overlay — explicitly NOT a reflexive 50/50 — and keep it only if it cuts maxDD without diluting Sharpe.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: The existing [[experiments/vrp-trend-book]] FAIL was a static short-vol leg reflexively blended 50/50 with trend — almost certainly a false-fail by trend dilution per the 2026-06-08 anti-pattern. This differs on three axes: (1) the CORE signal is the VIX/VIX3M term-structure regime switch (absent in the prior book) that exits short-vol before the spike crashes; (2) it is tested STANDALONE first per the new 'test every premium standalone, add hedge only as a small tail-overlay' lesson; (3) trend enters as a tail-sized overlay, never 50/50. It is also a different premium from the carry-family books that dominate the queue.
proposal.pairs_with: Validated 21-market crisis-alpha trend, as a SMALL tail overlay (opposite tails: VRP earns in calm/contango and crashes in vol spikes; trend pays the spike) — complementary-premia structure, not a reflexive blend.
proposal.gate0_data_check: Confirm FRED VXVCLS (3-Month VIX) exists with history back to ~2008 alongside VIXCLS; verify SVXY yfinance history and handle the Feb-2018 leverage change (-1x → -0.5x) which breaks return continuity — either start the sample 2018+ on the consistent -0.5x spec, or reconstruct a continuous short-vol return from shorting VXX (1x) and note the discontinuity. Verify the contango/backwardation switch is computable daily and the chosen ETF's daily returns carry the correct sign vs the VIX term-structure regime on known stress dates (Feb-2018 'Volmageddon', Mar-2020, 2022).

proposal.premium: Defensive / low-beta (Frazzini-Pedersen betting-against-beta — a leverage-aversion/lottery-demand RISK premium, NOT a forecast). This is the ONLY one of the five canonical AQR premia (Value/Momentum/Trend/Carry/Defensive) we have never tested: Value FAIL, Momentum FAIL, Trend VALIDATED, Carry NEAR-MISS — Defensive is the untouched corner.
proposal.market: US equities — survivorship-clean SMALL/MID-cap subset (where limits-to-arbitrage premia survive; the 2026-06-09 lesson showed large-liquid caps systematically NULL real cross-sectional premia).
proposal.data_source: Sharadar SEP (daily adj-close for returns + rolling beta + ADV liquidity floor), us_universe(marketcap='Small'/'Mid', include_delisted=True) for the survivorship-clean ranking universe, SF1 (marketcap, point-in-time via datekey) for size bucketing, and a broad market proxy (SPY via yf_panel, ETF — survivorship-safe) for the beta denominator.

For each name estimate 252-day rolling beta vs SPY, Vasicek-shrunk toward 1 (0.6·β̂ + 0.4·1) per Frazzini-Pedersen. Monthly rebalance: rank by shrunk beta; go LONG the low-beta half, SHORT the high-beta half with rank weights; lever the long leg up (×1/β_L) and de-lever the short leg (×1/β_H) so the book is ex-ante beta-neutral. Apply conservative small-cap costs (~25bps round-trip) plus a short-borrow haircut. Single pre-registered config (no grid), run end-to-end through research_integrity rails (CPCV, effective-N DSR, write-once holdout, deployment-sanity that it deploys a real diversified book, not 1-2 names).
proposal.why_not_duplicate: No experiment touches the Defensive/low-beta premium — the queue is dominated by carry, trend, and X+trend blends. It is NOT in the CLOSED list (which names only momentum/value/quality/technicals): BAB is a structurally distinct leverage-aversion premium. It also is NOT a re-run of net_share_issuance (different factor family AND different universe), and it directly executes the explicit 2026-06-09 lesson to test cross-sectional premia in SMALL/MID caps rather than large-liquid names. It is a risk premium with decades of real-money OOS evidence, not a prediction edge — respecting anti-pattern #4.
proposal.pairs_with: Tested STANDALONE first per the 2026-06-08 anti-pattern (no reflexive 50/50 trend blend). If it shows a real standalone edge, BAB is mildly ANTI-cyclical/defensive (low-beta outperforms in drawdowns), making it a candidate COMPLEMENTARY pro-cyclical-pair for the crypto-CARRY leg (an equity-defensive alternative to the futures-trend hedge) — added only as a small tail overlay if it cuts the carry drawdown WITHOUT diluting Sharpe.

Monthly rebalance. VALUE signal = book-to-market = bvps (ARQ, lagged to datekey filing date to kill look-ahead) / price; cross-sectional z-score. MOMENTUM signal = 12-1 total return (t-12→t-1, skip most recent month); cross-sectional z-score. Build each as a quintile long-short leg (long top quintile, short bottom quintile, equal-weight). PRIMARY (pre-registered verdict) = vol-matched 50/50 inverse-vol blend of the two long-short legs, rebalanced monthly, with realistic small/mid costs (25 bps round-trip). Report each leg STANDALONE, the leg-to-leg correlation, and the combined Sharpe/maxDD/MAR over the full SEP history incl. 2008/2020/2022 stress windows. Pre-registered success = combination Sharpe materially > each standalone AND maxDD cut vs the worse standalone (the carry+trend signature), with the holdout as the only arbiter.

This is NOT standalone (closed decision targets standalone factor hunting): it is the textbook COMPLEMENTARY pair whose strong NEGATIVE correlation is the diversification edge — the literal embodiment of pattern #1 ('the edge is the combination, not the signal') applied INSIDE equities for the first time. Every queued combination (vrp/credit/commod/reversal/crypto-carry × trend) uses TREND as the hedge leg; none is an intra-equity complementary pair. It is run in SMALL/MID caps because the freshest 2026-06-09 lesson proved limits-to-arbitrage premia (net-share-issuance) NULL in large-liquid and live in small/illiquid — so this is a genuinely new universe, not 'search harder' on a closed substrate.

proposal.title: Cross-asset VALUE (long-run 5-year reversal) across the 21-market futures basket × the validated TREND hedge — the canonical AQR value+momentum book we never tested
proposal.premium: Cross-asset value (long-horizon mean-reversion risk premium) combined with the validated trend / time-series-momentum crisis-alpha hedge — the textbook complementary, negatively-correlated AQR pair (value ≈ −0.5 corr to momentum/trend; the diversification IS the edge)
proposal.market: Cross-asset futures (equity indices, government bonds, commodities, FX) — the existing 21-market Boreas basket, 4 sectors, 21yr daily
proposal.data_source: yfinance continuous futures (the existing 21-market Boreas panel) + the built trend_returns() leg
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED/FREE ($0, no Gate-0 spend)
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN, pre-registered. (1) Build the VALUE leg STANDALONE first (per the 2026-06-08 over-blend lesson — never a reflexive 50/50). For each of the 21 markets compute value = −1 × trailing total return from t−60m to t−12m (skip the most recent 12m so it does NOT overlap the trend/momentum window; long-run reversal = AQR's cross-asset value proxy). Cross-sectionally rank and demean the value scores monthly, inverse-vol weight (trailing 60d vol), long cheap / short expensive, scale to a fixed ex-ante vol target, monthly rebalance, 8bps micro-cost (same rails as Boreas). (2) Run the standalone value leg through the shared research_integrity rails (search→write-once holdout→DSR/FDR→deployment-sanity). (3) ONLY if value clears its standalone search-sanity bar, add the validated trend leg as a SIZED overlay (grid the trend weight but report the PRE-REGISTERED primary = whatever minimises combined drawdown subject to no Sharpe dilution; the headline test is corr, drawdown-cut and MAR of value+trend, NOT a forced 50/50). CRITICAL construction note: value is built from RETURNS, which are correct on back-adjusted continuous series — do NOT use price-vs-moving-average levels (roll back-adjustment corrupts absolute price history).
proposal.why_not_duplicate: Trend has only ever been paired with CARRY (crypto/credit/commodity, all FAIL), VRP, and SHORT-term reversal (liquidity-provision, daily horizon, FAIL). Long-horizon cross-asset VALUE (5-yr reversal) is a structurally DIFFERENT premium and the canonical AQR complement to time-series momentum ('Value and Momentum Everywhere'), never tested here. It is NOT the closed equity cross-sectional value (different market = futures, different construction = long-run price reversal not B/M, capacity-rich not small-cap-bound). It is NOT short-term reversal (60m mean-reversion risk premium vs 1-week liquidity provision — opposite horizon and mechanism).
proposal.pairs_with: the validated 21-market trend leg (trend_returns) — value and trend/momentum are the most-documented negatively-correlated complementary pair with decades of real-money OOS; value standalone is weak post-2010 (hence medium, not high), but the diversification-as-edge claim is exactly what survived for carry+trend

Crucially it directly OBEYS the new 2026-06-09 meta-lesson (test in small/mid, not large-liquid) rather than re-running a closed large-cap substrate, and PEAD/SUE is deliberately avoided as already-flagged.

Only IF it shows a genuine standalone positive Sharpe with a real diversified book would I consider a SMALL trend tail-overlay (validated 21-market crisis-alpha hedge) sized to cut the short-leg squeeze risk in risk-on melt-ups — never a 50/50 that would dilute the premium.
proposal.gate0_data_check: 1) Confirm SF1 exposes cash-flow-from-operations (ncfo) and net income (netinc) and total assets (assets) as point-in-time fields with a usable datekey; if ncfo is missing, confirm the balance-sheet working-capital components (current assets/liabilities, cash, short-term debt) are present for the Sloan accrual definition. 2) Verify us_universe(marketcap='Small') and ='Mid' return a large enough cross-section (target ≥300 names/month with both fundamentals and SEP prices, delisted included) for stable quintiles. 3) Sanity-check accrual values on a known case (sign, magnitude vs assets) to confirm no split/scale artifact, exactly as the net-issuance post-mortem advises. 4) Confirm SEP volume is available to build the dollar-ADV tradability floor.

title: Post-Earnings-Announcement Drift (SUE) — mid-cap US long/short event-drift premium

proposal.title: Post-Earnings-Announcement Drift (SUE) — mid-cap US long/short event-drift premium
proposal.premium: PEAD / earnings-surprise drift — an information-diffusion / limits-to-arbitrage underreaction premium (distinct factor family from value/quality/momentum/issuance/accruals/BAB)
proposal.market: US equities, mid-cap (survivorship-clean), with small/large as generalization tiers
proposal.data_source: Sharadar SF1 (ARQ quarterly EPS, point-in-time via datekey) + Sharadar SEP (closeadj, delisted incl.), universe via us_universe(marketcap='Mid'); loaded with sf1()/sep_panel()
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED/FREE ($0, no Gate-0 risk — DATA_CATALOG explicitly states PEAD-on-SUE core is buildable on SF1+SEP)
proposal.signal_approach: For each name compute quarterly SUE = (EPS_q − EPS_{q−4}) / rolling-std of the prior 8 seasonal EPS changes, using SF1 ARQ EPS with datekey (filing date) as the strict as-of (never calendardate) to kill look-ahead. On the first trading day AFTER each filing, rank the universe into quintiles; go long the top-SUE quintile, short the bottom-SUE quintile, equal-weighted, hold each cohort for 60 trading days (overlapping cohorts → ~3 live at once), rebalanced monthly. Returns from SEP closeadj; vol-target the long/short book to 10% annual; report net of realistic mid-cap costs (15/25/50 bps). Pre-registered PRIMARY = mid-cap quintile L/S, 60-day hold, monthly rebalance, 25bps — that single config is the verdict (no grid cherry-pick). Test STANDALONE first; only if it clears search+holdout do I add the validated 21-market trend leg as a SMALL tail overlay (≤25% vol budget), kept only if it cuts maxDD WITHOUT diluting Sharpe (per the credit-carry reflexive-50/50 anti-pattern).
proposal.why_not_duplicate: PEAD has never actually been tested — the lone 'pead-pivot' experiment immediately pivoted to crypto funding-carry×trend and never built the SUE drift. The premia/momentum wiki page explicitly lists 'PEAD untested', and overview prior #3 names it as a live direction. It is a DIFFERENT factor family from every nulled equity factor: value/quality/issuance/accruals/BAB are slow valuation/balance-sheet/risk signals, whereas PEAD is a discrete EVENT-conditional information-diffusion drift — not a continuous cross-section, so it is not the same arbitraged substrate.
proposal.pairs_with: Validated 21-market crisis-alpha trend hedge as a SMALL tail overlay (not 50/50). PEAD long/short is broadly market-neutral but vulnerable to quant-deleveraging/correlation-spike crashes; trend's crisis-alpha smile is the complementary defensive tail — but added ONLY post-pass and sized to minimise drag.
proposal.gate0_data_check: (1) Confirm SF1 ARQ gives ≥8 prior quarters of EPS per mid-cap name to form the SUE std (drop names without history); (2) confirm datekey lags calendardate by a realistic filing gap and use datekey as as-of (spot-check 2-3 names); (3) confirm us_universe(marketcap='Mid', include_delisted=True) returns a survivorship-clean panel of sufficient breadth (≥150 names/quarter) so quintiles are diversified — guard against the 1-2-name deployment-mirage; (4) measure realised turnover and stress 25/50bps before concluding — low (quarterly-horizon, ~4x/yr) turnover is the cost defence vs the small-cap-cost-mirage lesson.
proposal.crowding_risk: medium — PEAD is published (Bernard-Thomas 1989) but is the rare anomaly that has NOT smoothly decayed; it persists precisely in less-covered, harder-to-arbitrage names (attention/illiquidity limits). The survivorship-clean mid-cap, low-turnover, event-conditional retail implementation is genuinely under-exploited vs the crowded continuous large-cap factor zoo.
proposal.generalization_plan: Broad universal mechanism (information diffusion / limits-to-arbitrage), so a mid-cap pass must GENERALISE. Pre-register the same frozen construction in UNTOUCHED cap tiers — small-cap (expect STRONGEST per limits-to-arbitrage, but re-check at 50bps realistic cost) and large-cap (expect WEAKEST/decayed) — and across 2-3 sectors. A real PEAD premium shows a MONOTONE limits-to-arbitrage gradient (small > mid > large) with broad OOS positivity; a single lucky tier + negatives elsewhere = overfit outlier, FAIL (the BAB cross-market lesson). Cross-tier breadth is the arbiter before any forward track.

title: Value × Momentum complementary combination in survivorship-clean US SMALL/MID caps — LONG-ONLY integrated-composite, cost-hardened variant (deployable-form mutation)

proposal.title: Value × Momentum complementary combination in survivorship-clean US SMALL/MID caps — LONG-ONLY integrated-composite, cost-hardened variant (deployable-form mutation)
proposal.premium: Same two complementary equity premia — value (pro-cyclical, mean-reversion) + cross-sectional momentum (trend-following), ~−0.4/−0.5 correlated (Asness/Moskowitz/Pedersen 'Value and Momentum Everywhere') — but captured at the NAME level via an integrated composite rank and harvested LONG-ONLY, so the diversification-as-edge is realized in a form a retail solo account can actually deploy (no short borrow).
proposal.market: US equities, SMALL + MID cap (survivorship-clean Sharadar), unlevered long-only cash equity
proposal.data_source: Sharadar SEP (sep_panel — adjusted prices for momentum + price denominator + volume for ADV floor), Sharadar SF1 (sf1 — bvps ARQ point-in-time via datekey for book-to-market), Sharadar TICKERS (us_universe marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True)
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED / FREE ($0, no Gate-0 spend risk)
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN (unchanged from parent): Universe = survivorship-clean US names via us_universe(marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True), tradability floor = 60-day median dollar-volume > $1M (SEP volume) to stay in the limits-to-arbitrage zone without being micro-junk. Monthly signal. VALUE = book-to-market = bvps(ARQ, lagged to datekey filing date to kill look-ahead) / price, cross-sectional z. MOMENTUM = 12-1 total return (t-12→t-1, skip last month), cross-sectional z. MUTATION (construction + deployment form): (1) INTEGRATE not isolate — collapse the two sleeves into ONE composite rank composite_z = 0.5·value_z + 0.5·momentum_z per name/month; this captures the negative value/momentum correlation at the NAME level and avoids the expensive-momentum / value-trap extremes a portfolio-of-portfolios would hold (the AQR integrated-combination result). (2) PRIMARY pre-registered, holdout-arbitrated form = LONG-ONLY top-tercile of composite_z, equal-weight, benchmarked vs the equal-weight small/mid universe (and a cap index proxy) — this DROPS the impractical small-cap short leg the parent's gate0 flagged. (3) TURNOVER HARDENING: monthly signal but rebalance with a no-trade band/hysteresis (enter only if composite rank ≤ 30th pct, exit only if it falls past 50th pct) plus optional 3-month signal smoothing; target < 60%/yr one-way turnover. (4) COST HARDENING: report net Sharpe at 25 / 50 / 75 bps round-trip (small/mid spread+impact), borrow cost = 0 (long-only) — verdict reads off the 50 bps case, not the optimistic point. DIAGNOSTIC ONLY (not the deploy verdict): still report each leg standalone, the leg-to-leg correlation, and the L/S quintile combo IN-SAMPLE to confirm the premium structurally exists — but the deployable book and the write-once holdout verdict are on the long-only cost-hardened composite. Pre-registered success = long-only composite tilt beats the equal-weight small/mid benchmark on Sharpe AND MAR net of 50 bps, survives 2008/2020/2022, and the negative value-momentum correlation visibly stabilizes the combined tilt vs a value-only or momentum-only long-only tilt; HOLDOUT is the only arbiter.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: atlas-equity-factors tested value AND momentum STANDALONE in LARGE-liquid caps → FAIL (edge-bound, closed). This is NOT standalone factor hunting: it is the textbook COMPLEMENTARY pair whose strong NEGATIVE correlation is the diversification edge (pattern #1) applied inside equities, in a genuinely new small/mid survivorship-clean substrate (the net-share-issuance lesson showed limits-to-arbitrage premia live in small/illiquid, dead in large-liquid). The mutation distinguishes it FURTHER from all closed work: it pre-registers a LONG-ONLY, capacity-realistic, cost-hardened deployable form rather than an undeployable small-cap L/S — fixing the exact deployment-sanity trap (#8) that turns paper Sharpe into a mirage.
proposal.pairs_with: Internally self-hedging (value pro-cyclical; momentum crashes on post-crash reversals — opposite tails). Long-only form is additive to the carry+trend book without short-borrow conflicts. Optional later: add a low-beta/defensive z or the validated 21-market trend leg as a SMALL crisis tail-overlay term in composite_z (NOT a reflexive 50/50, per the over-blend anti-pattern) only if it cuts combined drawdown without diluting Sharpe.
proposal.gate0_data_check: 1) Confirm us_universe(marketcap='Small') and 'Mid' each return enough survivorship-clean names (>500 small, >300 mid) with delisted included. 2) Confirm SF1 bvps point-in-time coverage for small/mid names, datekey (not calendardate) used as as-of. 3) Confirm SEP price+volume spans the same names with ≥12-month momentum lookback plus multi-decade stress (2008/2020/2022). 4) Confirm the $1M ADV floor leaves ≥100+ names so a top-tercile long book is genuinely diversified, not 1-2-name (deployment-sanity). 5) REVISED for long-only: shorting feasibility is now MOOT (no short leg) — instead verify (a) the equal-weight small/mid benchmark is reconstructable survivorship-clean, and (b) the no-trade band materially cuts turnover (measure realized one-way turnover) WITHOUT destroying the signal in-sample before committing the holdout.
proposal.crowding_risk: Value and momentum are the two most-published factors → crowded in large-liquid L/S quant books (Aug-2007 'quant quake' was a LEVERED-L/S deleveraging event). This variant sidesteps that channel: small/mid + tradability-floored is less arbitraged, and an UNLEVERED LONG-ONLY tilt has no short-crowding/forced-cover exposure and high $-capacity. Residual crowding risk is benchmark-relative tilt compression, monitored by tracking the live tercile spread vs history.
proposal.scope: Research/backtest over full SEP history (1998+) incl. 2008/2020/2022 stress; deployable form is unlevered long-only cash equity, retail-feasible from small AUM and scalable (long-only small/mid capacity is deep).
proposal.generalization_plan: The banded integrated-composite long-only template generalizes diversification-as-edge to ANY complementary signal pair WITHOUT needing a short book — future complementary legs (defensive/low-beta, trend tail-overlay) slot in as additional terms in composite_z. Robustness battery before holdout: (a) small vs mid buckets separately, (b) net Sharpe across 25/50/75 bps, (c) no-trade band width sensitivity, (d) 50/50 vs alternative leg weights — to prove the result is a structural premium, not a single-parameter artifact, with the write-once holdout as the sole arbiter.

title: Crypto idiosyncratic-skewness (lottery) premium — market-neutral short-high-skew / long-low-skew basket

proposal.title: Crypto idiosyncratic-skewness (lottery) premium — market-neutral short-high-skew / long-low-skew basket
proposal.premium: Skewness / lottery-preference behavioral risk premium: investors structurally overpay for positive-skew 'moonshot' payoffs, so high-skew assets are overpriced (low future returns) and low/negative-skew 'boring' assets are underpriced. You get paid to bear the un-fun, low-lottery exposure that speculators shun. Distinct premium family from carry / trend / momentum / value / BAB (a higher-moment, not first/second-moment, effect).
proposal.market: Crypto perpetual-swap universe — top ~40 liquid coins with tradable perps on Binance/Bybit (the least-arbitraged corner the wiki repeatedly points to, and the structural epicentre of retail lottery demand: ICO/memecoin/alt-season culture).
proposal.data_source: Binance/Bybit public API — daily spot/perp prices + funding (same free source already used for the Midas carry leg). Realized idiosyncratic skewness computed from trailing daily returns.
proposal.free_or_owned: owned/free (Binance/Bybit public API; no paid/gated data).
proposal.signal_approach: Universe: top ~40 coins by trailing 30-day perp dollar-volume (liquid + perp-tradable on both legs). Weekly rebalance: for each coin regress its past-60-day daily returns on an equal-weight crypto-market return and take the RESIDUAL skewness (idiosyncratic, de-market). Rank cross-sectionally; SHORT the top tercile (highest positive idiosyncratic skew = lottery/moonshot names) and LONG the bottom tercile (lowest/most-negative skew = established coins), dollar-neutral and BTC-beta-neutral, inverse-vol weighted within each leg. Hold one week. Apply realistic crypto perp taker fees (~5bps) + slippage + short funding cost. Pre-register the primary config; evaluate net STANDALONE Sharpe/DD through the research_integrity rails (CPCV / PBO / write-once holdout). Only if it stands alone do we test the validated 21-market trend as a SMALL tail overlay (never reflexive 50/50).
proposal.why_not_duplicate: Skewness/lottery is a brand-new premium family — 0 prior experiments. It is NOT the failed reversal-trend-book (that was FUTURES short-term reversal + trend; different market AND first-moment mechanism), NOT Midas (illiquidity+carry pair), NOT crypto-carry-trend (funding carry), NOT any equity factor null (those are value/momentum/quality/BAB/accruals/issuance — none is a higher-moment skewness sort). Different premium, different market, tested standalone.
proposal.pairs_with: The validated 21-market crisis-alpha trend leg, as a SMALL tail overlay only (opposite tails: short-skew is pro-cyclical — it bleeds in speculative alt-season melt-ups when lottery names rip, and earns as speculation normalises/collapses; trend is crisis-alpha). Per the 2026-06-08 anti-pattern: test skewness STANDALONE first; add trend ONLY if it cuts the tail WITHOUT diluting Sharpe; size to minimise drag, never 50/50.
proposal.gate0_data_check: (1) Survivorship — build a delisted-inclusive multi-coin daily panel; if dead/delisted coins are dropped, the low-skew/'established' long leg is biased upward (survivorship inflates the premium) — verify dead coins are retained or quantify the bias. (2) Short-leg tradability — confirm liquid perps exist across the WHOLE sorted universe, especially the high-skew shorts (the strongest lottery effect lives in tiny un-shortable coins; the tradable version is mid-tier alts — confirm skewness still varies meaningfully within the perp-tradable set). (3) History — perps launched ~2019-2020, so ~5-6yr; confirm the sample spans ≥1 alt-season mania AND ≥1 crash (else regime-concentrated like the Midas crypto caveat). (4) Costs — stress realistic taker fees (~5-10bps) + slippage + short funding/borrow; weekly turnover must survive. (5) Estimability — 60-90d window gives enough daily obs for a stable idiosyncratic-skew estimate.
proposal.crowding_risk: low — crypto skewness/lottery is barely studied or arbitraged versus the heavily-published equity factor zoo; retail lottery demand in crypto is extreme, structural, and persistently un-arbitraged (smart money structurally isn't shorting memecoins at scale).
proposal.generalization_plan: Lottery-preference is a universal behavioral mechanism (documented in equities — Bali/Cakici/Whitelaw MAX, Boyer-Mitton-Vorkink expected-skewness — and equity options). PRIMARY harvest is crypto, where the demand is strongest and arbitrage weakest. Generalisation check (untouched, clean data): run the same idiosyncratic-skew/MAX sort in US SMALL/MID-cap equities via survivorship-clean SEP — a real premium should show at least weak OOS positivity there too (sector-neutral, realistic costs). If it is crypto-ONLY and nulls in equities, demote to LOCAL and justify via the crypto-specific structural retail-lottery story, with forward-demo confirmation as the arbiter (mirroring the Midas forward gate).

title: VARIANT (robustness mutation): LONG-ONLY, SECURITY-LEVEL-INTEGRATED Value+Momentum composite with buffered low-turnover rebalance in survivorship-clean US SMALL/MID caps — drops the impractical small-cap SHORT leg as the capacity-realistic deployment form

proposal.title: VARIANT (robustness mutation): LONG-ONLY, SECURITY-LEVEL-INTEGRATED Value+Momentum composite with buffered low-turnover rebalance in survivorship-clean US SMALL/MID caps — drops the impractical small-cap SHORT leg as the capacity-realistic deployment form
proposal.premium: Same two complementary equity premia — value (pro-cyclical, mean-reversion) + cross-sectional momentum (trend-following), ~-0.4/-0.5 leg correlation = the diversification edge (Asness/Moskowitz/Pedersen 'Value and Momentum Everywhere'). MUTATION captures that same negative correlation via Asness/Israel/Moskowitz INTEGRATION (combine scores at the stock level, sort once) rather than portfolio-level mixing, and harvests it LONG-ONLY so the edge survives the brutal small-cap borrow wall.
proposal.market: US equities, SMALL + MID cap (survivorship-clean Sharadar), long-only.
proposal.data_source: Sharadar SEP (sep_panel — adjusted prices for momentum + price denominator for B/M, plus volume for the ADV floor), Sharadar SF1 (sf1 — bvps ARQ point-in-time via datekey for book-to-market), Sharadar TICKERS (us_universe marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True).
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED / FREE ($0, no Gate-0 spend risk)
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN. Universe = survivorship-clean us_universe(marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True), filtered to a tradability floor (60-day median dollar-volume > $1M from SEP volume). VALUE z = book-to-market = bvps(ARQ, lagged to datekey) / price, cross-sectional z-score. MOMENTUM z = 12-1 total return (t-12→t-1, skip last month), cross-sectional z-score. **KEY MUTATION 1 — integration not mixing:** combine into ONE composite score per name (equal-weight z_value + z_momentum, computed per name BEFORE sorting), then sort the universe ONCE on the composite — this avoids holding names that are cheap-but-falling or expensive-but-rising and is the documented lower-turnover, higher-Sharpe form. **KEY MUTATION 2 — long-only, capacity-realistic:** PRIMARY pre-registered book = LONG the top tercile of the composite, equal-weight, vs a passive small/mid universe benchmark (drop the short leg entirely — it is the single fragile part: small-cap borrow is unavailable/expensive/recall-prone and a small-cap long-short backtest is a deployment mirage). **KEY MUTATION 3 — buffered low-turnover rebalance:** compute signals monthly but trade with a hysteresis band (ENTER on top-30%, EXIT only when a name falls out of top-50%) to cut momentum's churn; this is the cost-hardening fix. **KEY MUTATION 4 — cost hardening:** report net results at 25/50/75 bps round-trip (small-cap realistic), borrow cost = 0 (long-only), and a 5-day participation-rate fill check against ADV. Pre-registered success = the integrated long-only composite beats BOTH standalone long-only legs (value-only tercile, momentum-only tercile) on Sharpe AND cuts maxDD vs the worse standalone (the carry+trend signature, demonstrated long-only) over full SEP history incl. 2008/2020/2022, AND the value-leg vs momentum-leg return correlation prints negative (mechanism intact). Long-short is reported ONLY as a non-deployable upper-bound diagnostic. Holdout is the sole arbiter.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: atlas-equity-factors tested value AND momentum STANDALONE in LARGE-liquid caps → FAIL (closed). This is the COMPLEMENTARY pair (negative-correlation diversification = the edge) applied INSIDE equities, in a genuinely new small/mid substrate where limits-to-arbitrage premia were just shown to live (net-share-issuance null in large, live in small). The mutation vs the parent elite is purely a ROBUSTNESS/DEPLOYABILITY fix: integration-not-mixing, long-only-not-long-short (kills the small-cap-short mirage flagged in the parent's own gate0 item #5), and buffered turnover — it does not change the thesis, it makes the SAME thesis fundable.
proposal.pairs_with: Internally self-hedging (value pro-cyclical; momentum crashes on post-crash reversals — opposite tails, and integration lets value's strength offset momentum's crash in the SAME names). Optional later, per the 2026-06-08 over-blend anti-pattern: overlay the validated 21-market diversified TREND leg as a SMALL crisis tail-hedge sleeve (NOT reflexive 50/50) only if it cuts the combined book's drawdown without diluting Sharpe.
proposal.gate0_data_check: 1) us_universe(marketcap='Small') and 'Mid' each return enough survivorship-clean names (target >500 small, >300 mid), delisted included. 2) SF1 bvps point-in-time coverage adequate for small/mid; datekey (not calendardate) as as-of. 3) SEP price+volume coverage spans the same names with ≥12m momentum lookback + multi-decade stress (2008/2020/2022). 4) After the $1M ADV floor AND the top-30% tercile, the deployed long book still holds ≥100 names (deployment-sanity: must be a real diversified book, not a 1-2 name artifact). 5) Long-only removes the short-feasibility blocker entirely — confirm borrow is NOT in the cost path; the only realism gate left is participation-rate fillability of the long tercile at $5K–$1M sizing, which the $1M ADV floor should clear.
proposal.crowding_risk: Value & momentum are the most-studied equity factors (crowded in large-cap), but (a) the small/mid substrate is structurally less arbitraged, (b) long-only avoids the crowded short-squeeze / hard-to-borrow tail, and (c) the integrated composite is a less-replicated construction than naive single-factor sorts. Residual risk: momentum-crowding-driven crash reversals — mitigated by the value leg inside the same composite. Capacity is finite (small-cap) but ample for a single account growing into it, and the goal is account growth not institutional AUM.
proposal.scope: US small+mid cap equities, long-only, monthly signal with buffered (hysteresis-band) rebalance, full Sharadar SEP history with explicit 2008/2020/2022 stress windows. Both cap tiers must work, not just one.
proposal.generalization_plan: Promote ONLY if the integrated long-only composite beats both standalone long-only legs on Sharpe AND cuts maxDD across ALL of: (a) cap tiers tested separately (small vs mid — reject if it only works in one), (b) sub-periods (pre-2010 vs post-2010, to survive momentum's weak 2011-2020 decade), (c) cost levels 25/50/75 bps, (d) sector-neutral vs raw composite (must not be a single-sector bet). The write-once holdout is the sole arbiter; in-search OOS is treated as contaminated. Long-short remains a diagnostic upper bound only. If it clears, optionally test the small diversified-trend tail-overlay as a separate gated step.

title: Crypto-native time-series momentum (trend) as a STANDALONE crisis-alpha premium in the less-arbitraged crypto corner

proposal.title: Crypto-native time-series momentum (trend) as a STANDALONE crisis-alpha premium in the less-arbitraged crypto corner
proposal.premium: Trend / time-series momentum (crisis-alpha, defensive tail) — tested for a POSITIVE *standalone* premium specifically where arbitrage has not yet arrived (crypto), unlike efficient DM futures where it is empirically ~0.
proposal.market: Crypto — a diversified basket of the most-liquid USDT perpetual futures (BTC, ETH + major alts), NOT single-name and NOT the DM futures basket.
proposal.data_source: Binance/Bybit public API (daily klines + funding rates) via the existing crypto adapters already used to build carry_returns().
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED/FREE ($0 — same crypto pipe as the Midas carry leg).
proposal.signal_approach: Universe = top-15 USDT perps by trailing ADV (INCLUDING delisted/dead perps to fight survivorship), rebalanced weekly. Per coin, signal = sign of an equal-weight blend of trailing 1m/3m/6m total returns (long if blend>0, short if <0); position size = inverse-realised-vol scaled to a fixed per-coin vol target; aggregate to equal-risk, cap gross leverage at 2x. Costs modeled at 10bps taker + realised funding paid/earned on the perp. PRE-REGISTERED PRIMARY = the 1/3/6m sign blend, weekly, top-15-ADV, 10bps; everything else is a robustness check, the primary is the verdict. Tested STANDALONE first (per the anti-pattern); NO reflexive 50/50 blend with anything.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: Distinct from [[boreas-tsmom]] (DM/efficient FUTURES, where standalone trend is ~0 and only the HEDGE survives) — this tests whether the standalone premium SURVIVES in a young, retail-dominated, under-arbitraged market. Distinct from [[crypto-carry-trend-book]] and the carry×trend books, which all used the 21-market FUTURES trend basket as the hedge leg, not crypto-NATIVE trend. Distinct from the closed '21→48 broadening' decision, where crypto was a small whipsaw-y slice DILUTING a clean futures core — here it is a dedicated crypto strategy with crypto-appropriate (faster, vol-scaled) parameters. No existing experiment tests crypto-native TS-momentum as a standalone premium.
proposal.pairs_with: The near-miss crypto funding-carry (Midas) leg — opposite tails WITHIN the same least-arbitraged corner (carry crashes when crypto crashes; crypto trend goes short and pays in a sustained crash). IF this validates AND carry passes its 2026-08-28 forward verdict, the two compose a SELF-CONTAINED crypto two-premium book (diversification-as-edge, all in the best corner). Explicitly: tested standalone first, sized later as a tail-overlay if-and-only-if it cuts the carry tail WITHOUT diluting Sharpe — NEVER a reflexive 50/50, and NOT re-opening Midas in-sample.
proposal.gate0_data_check: (1) Verify Binance/Bybit kline+funding history depth PER coin and the available perp universe — confirm ≥~5–6yr (covers 2021 bull + 2022 bear = ≥2 distinct regimes). (2) CRITICAL: confirm we can include delisted/dead perps (or explicitly document the survivorship bias and bound its impact) — crypto has heavy delisting churn. (3) Confirm taker-fee (10bps) + realised funding are both modeled in net returns. (4) Heed the 21→48 caution: pre-commit to reporting whether the result is BTC/ETH-only or holds across the alt basket (whipsaw risk is real in alts).
proposal.crowding_risk: medium — crypto trend-following is published (crypto-CTA products exist), but the ALT corner remains under-arbitraged vs DM futures; low turnover (weekly) keeps it robust to the realistic-cost trap that killed small-cap BAB.
proposal.generalization_plan: The trend MECHANISM is universal, but the CLAIM here is deliberately local: a standalone-POSITIVE trend premium that survives ONLY where institutional arbitrage hasn't arrived — which is exactly why it is ~0 in efficient DM futures and (hypothesised) >0 in crypto. Economic reason it lives here: young, retail-dominated, capital-constrained market with strong reflexive trends and slow arb capital. Confirm robustness WITHIN crypto: (a) result must hold across the alt basket, not just BTC/ETH; (b) positive in BOTH the 2021 bull and 2022 bear sub-samples; (c) survivorship-robust (dead perps included); (d) net of realistic taker+funding costs. Then forward-validate on fresh crypto data (the corner's only honest arbiter given the short/regime-concentrated sample), exactly as the carry leg is being forward-validated to 2026-08-28.

title: Value × Momentum complementary combination in survivorship-clean US SMALL/MID caps — LONG-ONLY integrated-composite, cost-hardened, with a broad-market TREND REGIME-OVERLAY (crisis-defended deployable variant)

proposal.title: Value × Momentum complementary combination in survivorship-clean US SMALL/MID caps — LONG-ONLY integrated-composite, cost-hardened, with a broad-market TREND REGIME-OVERLAY (crisis-defended deployable variant)
proposal.premium: Same two complementary equity premia captured at the name level via an integrated composite rank (value = pro-cyclical mean-reversion + cross-sectional momentum = trend-following, ~−0.4/−0.5 correlated, Asness/Moskowitz/Pedersen) and harvested LONG-ONLY so the diversification-as-edge deploys without short borrow — but now wrapped in a single, mechanical broad-market TIME-SERIES-TREND (absolute-momentum) exposure scaler. The wrapper imports the house's already-VALIDATED trend crisis-alpha (BOREAS: trend has no standalone premium but is a near-mechanical defensive hedge that ~halves drawdown) as an EXPOSURE OVERLAY rather than a separate book. Rationale: a beta≈1 long-only small/mid tilt structurally CANNOT clear the house drawdown bar (a long-only small/mid book lost ~50% in 2008; 'fundable' at this firm means ~14% DD, not 50%), so the binding fragility of the parent is crisis drawdown, not signal — the overlay fixes exactly that.
proposal.market: US equities, SMALL + MID cap (survivorship-clean Sharadar), unlevered long-only cash equity, with NET equity exposure scaled 0–100% by the trend overlay (de-risked capital parked in cash / T-bill proxy at ~risk-free, never shorted, never levered).
proposal.data_source: Sharadar SEP (sep_panel — adjusted prices for momentum + price denominator + volume for ADV floor + the broad-market reference series for the trend overlay), Sharadar SF1 (sf1 — bvps ARQ point-in-time via datekey for book-to-market), Sharadar TICKERS (us_universe marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True). Trend-overlay reference = the equal-weight small/mid universe price series (reconstructed survivorship-clean from SEP) and, as a robustness cross-check, a cap-index proxy from SEP — both OWNED.
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED / FREE ($0, no Gate-0 spend risk)
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN from parent (unchanged): Universe = survivorship-clean US names via us_universe(marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True); tradability floor = 60-day median dollar-volume > $1M; monthly signal; VALUE = bvps(ARQ, lagged to datekey)/price, cross-sectional z; MOMENTUM = 12-1 total return (skip last month), cross-sectional z; INTEGRATED composite_z = 0.5·value_z + 0.5·momentum_z per name/month; deployable book = LONG-ONLY top-tercile of composite_z, equal-weight; turnover hardening via no-trade band (enter ≤30th pct, exit >50th pct) + optional 3-month signal smoothing, target <60%/yr one-way; cost-hardening net Sharpe at 25/50/75 bps round-trip, verdict reads off the 50 bps case. NEW — TREND REGIME OVERLAY (the mutation), kept deliberately simple to avoid the over-fit/over-blend anti-pattern: (a) compute a SLOW broad-market trend state monthly = reference price vs its trailing 10-month moving average (the classic Faber/absolute-momentum rule) — ONE pre-registered frozen threshold; (b) ABOVE trend → hold the composite book at 100% net exposure; BELOW trend → scale net exposure DOWN to a frozen 50% (graded/binary-to-cash variants reported only as a small robustness grid, NOT optimized to a point); (c) de-risked capital earns the cash/T-bill proxy; (d) because the MA is slow and evaluated monthly the overlay flips rarely (count flips 1998–2024 to confirm), so its added turnover/cost is small and is INCLUDED in the 50 bps net figures. DIAGNOSTIC (not the verdict): report the book WITH and WITHOUT the overlay to isolate the crisis-alpha contribution and confirm the BOREAS pattern — drawdown CUT at little-to-no Sharpe cost (the carry+trend result: −45% DD at 0.66→0.65 Sharpe). Also still report each leg standalone, leg-to-leg correlation, and the in-sample L/S quintile combo to confirm the premium structurally exists. Pre-registered success = overlaid long-only composite beats the equal-weight small/mid benchmark on Sharpe AND MAR net of 50 bps, MATERIALLY cuts the 2008/2020/2022 drawdown vs the un-overlaid book (the deploy-relevant fix), and the negative value-momentum correlation still visibly stabilizes the combined tilt; the write-once HOLDOUT is the SOLE arbiter.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: atlas-equity-factors tested value AND momentum STANDALONE in LARGE-liquid caps → FAIL (edge-bound, closed). This is the textbook COMPLEMENTARY pair (strong negative correlation = the diversification edge, pattern #1/#2) inside equities, in a new small/mid survivorship-clean substrate where limits-to-arbitrage premia survive. It distinguishes itself FURTHER from the parent and all closed work by pre-registering a LONG-ONLY, capacity-realistic, cost-hardened form AND by importing the firm's VALIDATED 21-market trend crisis-alpha as an exposure scaler — so the deploy fragility the parent left open (a 50% crisis DD on a beta-1 long-only tilt) is fixed with a mechanism the firm has already proven works, not a new untested idea. It is NOT trend-as-standalone (BOREAS proved that's worthless) — trend is used ONLY as a defensive exposure overlay, exactly the validated role.
proposal.pairs_with: Internally self-hedging (value pro-cyclical; momentum crashes on post-crash reversals — opposite tails) AND now externally crisis-defended by the trend overlay. Long-only + overlay form is additive to the carry+trend book with no short-borrow conflict; the overlay shares the SAME trend crisis-alpha mechanism already in the book, so it reinforces rather than duplicates risk. Optional later: a low-beta/defensive z as a SMALL additional term in composite_z (NOT a reflexive 50/50) only if it further cuts combined drawdown without diluting Sharpe.
proposal.gate0_data_check: 1) us_universe(marketcap='Small') and 'Mid' each return enough survivorship-clean names (>500 small, >300 mid), delisted included. 2) SF1 bvps point-in-time coverage for small/mid names, datekey (not calendardate) as as-of. 3) SEP price+volume spans the same names with ≥12-month momentum lookback plus 2008/2020/2022 stress. 4) $1M ADV floor leaves ≥100+ names so the top-tercile book is genuinely diversified (deployment-sanity). 5) Long-only ⇒ shorting moot; instead verify (a) the equal-weight small/mid benchmark is reconstructable survivorship-clean and (b) the no-trade band materially cuts turnover in-sample without destroying signal. 6) NEW for the overlay: (a) confirm the broad-market reference series (equal-weight universe price + cap-index proxy) is reconstructable over the FULL 10-month-MA window from 1998+; (b) confirm a cash/T-bill return proxy is available for de-risked periods (or default to 0% conservatively); (c) COUNT regime flips 1998–2024 to confirm the overlay is slow (low added turnover/cost) BEFORE committing the holdout.
proposal.crowding_risk: Value/momentum are the two most-published factors → crowded in large-liquid LEVERED-L/S quant books (the Aug-2007 quake was a levered-L/S deleveraging event). This variant sidesteps that channel: small/mid + tradability-floored is less arbitraged, and an UNLEVERED LONG-ONLY tilt has no short-crowding/forced-cover exposure with deep $-capacity. The trend overlay is a mechanical risk-management layer (uncrowdable, no capacity drag). Residual risk = benchmark-relative tilt compression, monitored via the live tercile spread vs history; and overlay whipsaw in choppy non-trending markets (bounded by the slow MA + monthly cadence, and explicitly measured by the with/without-overlay diagnostic).
proposal.scope: Research/backtest over full SEP history (1998+) incl. 2008/2020/2022 stress; deployable form is unlevered long-only cash equity with net exposure scaled 0–100% by the trend overlay, retail-feasible from small AUM and scalable (long-only small/mid + cash overlay has deep capacity).
proposal.generalization_plan: The banded integrated-composite long-only template PLUS a slow trend exposure-overlay generalizes 'diversification-as-edge' to ANY complementary signal pair WITHOUT a short book AND gives any long-only premium book a reusable, mechanical crisis-defense layer. Future complementary legs (defensive/low-beta) slot in as composite_z terms; the overlay slots onto any long-only sleeve. Robustness battery before the holdout: (a) small vs mid buckets separately, (b) net Sharpe across 25/50/75 bps, (c) no-trade band width sensitivity, (d) 50/50 vs alternative leg weights, (e) NEW — overlay sensitivity: 10-month MA vs 200-day MA vs 12-month absolute-momentum, and de-risk-to-50% vs to-cash, reported as a SMALL frozen grid to prove the drawdown cut is structural (not a single-parameter artifact). Write-once holdout is the sole arbiter; success requires the overlay to cut crisis drawdown at little-to-no Sharpe cost, mirroring the validated carry+trend result.
proposal.mutation_note: MUTATION = added a single, frozen, slow broad-market TREND REGIME-OVERLAY (10-month-MA absolute-momentum, de-risk to 50%/cash below trend) that scales the long-only book's net equity exposure. Keeps everything that worked (integrated composite, long-only deployability, turnover band, cost-hardening) and fixes the parent's binding fragility — a beta-1 long-only tilt's un-fundable ~50% crisis drawdown — by importing the firm's already-validated trend crisis-alpha as an exposure scaler rather than a standalone book.
