title: Value × Momentum complementary combination in US SMALL/MID caps — LONG-ONLY, turnover-buffered, deployment-realistic variant (diversification-as-edge, no-borrow form)

proposal.title: Value × Momentum complementary combination in US SMALL/MID caps — LONG-ONLY, turnover-buffered, deployment-realistic variant (diversification-as-edge, no-borrow form)
proposal.premium: Same two complementary equity risk premia — value (pro-cyclical, mean-reversion) + cross-sectional momentum (trend-following) — whose ~−0.4/−0.5 leg correlation is the edge (Asness/Moskowitz/Pedersen 'Value and Momentum Everywhere'). Mutation: capture the combination as a LONG-ONLY tilt vs the tradable universe instead of a small-cap long-short, because (a) small/mid borrow is expensive/often unavailable so the L/S form is largely undeployable (anti-pattern #8 deployment-sanity), and (b) the combination's diversification benefit survives on the long side, where AQR-style funds actually run it.
proposal.market: US equities, SMALL + MID cap (survivorship-clean Sharadar)
proposal.data_source: Sharadar SEP (sep_panel — adjusted prices for momentum + denominator, volume for ADV floor), Sharadar SF1 (sf1 — bvps ARQ point-in-time via datekey for book-to-market), Sharadar TICKERS (us_universe marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True). Unchanged from parent — no new/gated data.
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED / FREE ($0, no Gate-0 spend risk)
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN. Universe = survivorship-clean us_universe(marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True), filtered to a tradability floor (60-day median dollar-volume > $1M from SEP volume). Monthly. SIGNALS (unchanged): VALUE = book-to-market = bvps(ARQ, lagged to datekey) / price, cross-sectional z; MOMENTUM = 12-1 total return (t-12→t-1, skip last month), cross-sectional z. Blend = vol-matched/equal-risk average of the two z-scores into ONE composite cross-sectional score. CONSTRUCTION MUTATION (the deployable verdict): build a LONG-ONLY tilt = overweight the top-tercile-by-composite-score, equal-weight (or mildly score-tilted), benchmarked to the equal-weight tradable small/mid universe; report it as an active tilt (IR, Sharpe, maxDD, MAR vs the EW-universe benchmark). TURNOVER-BUFFER / COST-HARDENING: hysteresis no-trade band — a holding only EXITS when its composite rank falls below the 50th pct and only ENTERS above the 70th pct, plus a hard cap on monthly turnover (≤15%) — to keep momentum churn and realistic small/mid costs bounded; cost assumption raised to a conservative 35–40 bps round-trip. DIAGNOSTICS ONLY (not the verdict): also compute the dollar-neutral quintile long-short legs to confirm each standalone premium, the ~−0.4/−0.5 leg-to-leg correlation, and that the combination cuts DD vs the worse standalone (the carry+trend signature). PRE-REGISTERED success = the LONG-ONLY combined tilt beats BOTH single-factor long-only tilts on risk-adjusted terms (higher IR/Sharpe, lower maxDD) net of 35–40 bps with the turnover band ON, across full SEP history incl. 2008/2020/2022; holdout is the only arbiter.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: atlas-equity-factors tested value AND momentum STANDALONE in LARGE-liquid caps → FAIL (edge-bound, closed). This is the textbook COMPLEMENTARY pair (pattern #1: edge is the combination) inside SMALL/MID caps (limits-to-arbitrage corner, pattern #4), and this variant additionally fixes the parent's deployability hole: it pre-registers the no-borrow LONG-ONLY tilt as the verdict, so a 'PROMOTE' means a genuinely tradable book — not a small-cap-short mirage (anti-pattern #8) — making it both more robust and capacity-honest (pattern #5).
proposal.pairs_with: Internally self-hedging (value pro-cyclical, momentum crashes on post-crash reversals — opposite tails). Optional later: overlay the validated 21-market trend leg as a SMALL crisis tail-overlay (NOT reflexive 50/50, per the 2026-06-08 over-blend anti-pattern) only if it cuts the combined book's drawdown without diluting Sharpe.
proposal.gate0_data_check: 1) us_universe(marketcap='Small') and 'Mid' each return enough survivorship-clean delisted-included names (target >500 small, >300 mid). 2) SF1 bvps point-in-time coverage adequate for small/mid, datekey (not calendardate) used as as-of. 3) SEP price+volume spans the same names with 12m momentum lookback + 2008/2020/2022 stress. 4) ADV floor leaves ≥100 names/side so terciles are diversified. 5) SHORTING CONCERN RESOLVED: long-only tilt is the primary deployable form, so no small-cap borrow assumption is needed; instead verify (a) the EW tradable-universe benchmark is constructed survivorship-clean, (b) the turnover band actually holds annual turnover and net-of-40bps results stable, (c) the L/S diagnostics still show the negative correlation (else the long-only combination benefit is suspect).
proposal.crowding_risk: Value and momentum are the two most-studied equity factors, so crowding is real — but concentrated in LARGE-liquid caps and in the L/S form; the small/mid long-only corner is structurally less arbitraged (pattern #4) and the EDGE here is the NEGATIVE LEG CORRELATION (a structural diversification property), which is not itself crowdable away the way a single mispricing is. Main residual risk: long-only momentum tilt overlaps with crowded retail/ETF momentum flows — monitored via the cost-curve and capacity checks, not assumed away.
proposal.scope: Single asset class (US equities, small+mid cap), monthly rebalance, long-only tilt vs EW universe benchmark, owned/free Sharadar data, full multi-decade SEP history incl. three named stress windows. Deliberately narrow: one universe, one construction, two frozen signals — no parameter sweep beyond the pre-registered turnover band.
proposal.generalization_plan: 1) OUT-OF-UNIVERSE: rerun the identical construction on SMALL-only and MID-only separately — combination benefit should persist in both, not just the pooled set. 2) SIGNAL ROBUSTNESS: as a non-verdict check, swap single-ratio B/M for a composite value (B/M + E/P + FCF-yield + sales-yield from SF1) and confirm the long-only combination edge is not a B/M artifact (value's known 2007–2020 fragility). 3) SUB-PERIOD STABILITY: report rolling 3yr IR and the three stress windows separately; require positive contribution in ≥2 of 3 regimes. 4) COST/TURNOVER STRESS: sweep the no-trade band (50/70 → 40/60, 60/80) and costs (25→50 bps) — the verdict must survive the conservative corner. 5) HOLDOUT: a single write-once holdout (last ~5yr) is the only arbiter; in-search OOS treated as contaminated (pattern #7).

title: Crypto cross-sectional LOW-RISK (lottery-demand) premium — delta-neutral long low-vol / short high-vol coins, in the least-arbitraged corner

proposal.title: Crypto cross-sectional LOW-RISK (lottery-demand) premium — delta-neutral long low-vol / short high-vol coins, in the least-arbitraged corner
proposal.premium: Low-volatility / lottery-demand behavioral risk premium (you are paid to NOT hold over-priced 'lottery' assets). Distinct from beta (BAB) — sorts on idiosyncratic/total realized vol and MAX lottery-payoff demand.
proposal.market: Crypto spot (multi-coin cross-section, dollar-neutral long-short, BTC-beta-hedged)
proposal.data_source: Binance/Bybit public spot daily close+volume for a point-in-time multi-coin universe; BTC as market-beta hedge. Reuses the existing crypto data infra behind carry_returns().
proposal.free_or_owned: owned/free — Binance/Bybit public spot API (listed in DATA CATALOG)
proposal.signal_approach: Universe: top ~60 crypto assets by trailing-30d USD volume on Binance spot, reconstituted monthly, survivorship-aware (include coins later delisted; if delisted-coin history is unavailable, FIX the universe to coins listed as of sample start and hold dead coins to their last-trade date — no look-ahead). Signal per coin: 30-day trailing realized volatility (daily log returns) AND the Bali-Cakici-Whitelaw MAX (mean of the 5 largest daily returns over the past 21d) as the lottery proxy; combine into a cross-sectional low-risk rank. Book: monthly-rebalanced, dollar-neutral long-short — LONG bottom-tercile (lowest vol/MAX) coins, SHORT top-tercile (highest vol/lottery), inverse-vol weighted within each leg, then BTC-beta-neutralised (offset residual market beta so only the cross-sectional low-risk premium remains). Frozen costs: stress realistic crypto spot taker fees (~10–30bps round-trip) + alt short-borrow/funding. Verdict requires write-once HOLDOUT PASS, an MCPT beat (permutation null = random low/high split of same coins), and excess Sharpe over a long-only inverse-vol crypto book.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: Distinct from crypto-native-TSMOM (momentum, FAIL) and from crypto funding/basis-carry (the Midas near-miss — a carry/basis mechanism, not cross-sectional risk). It is also NOT a re-open of the closed EQUITY BAB/low-vol work: that is a different MARKET and this is the legitimate anti-pattern-#3 move (change the axis market/premium-type, not the parameters). The mechanism that died in crowded liquid US equities is here tested where arbitrage is structurally limited.
proposal.pairs_with: Standalone FIRST (per the 2026-06-08 reflexive-blend lesson). Only if it survives, add the validated 21-market trend leg as a SMALL tail overlay (≤25%, sized to cut crypto-crash drawdown without diluting Sharpe) — NEVER a 50/50 blend. Complementary tails: low-vol/lottery is pro-cyclical (short-high-vol leg bleeds in alt-season melt-ups), trend is crisis-alpha for the crashes.
proposal.gate0_data_check: Verify a SURVIVORSHIP-AWARE coin panel: can we pull daily history for coins LATER DELISTED on Binance (klines often retain delisted symbols)? If yes → reconstitute monthly incl. dead coins; if not → fixed start-universe held to each coin's last-trade date, state the residual caveat. Confirm ≥30-coin cross-sectional breadth back to ≥2019 spanning ≥2 regimes (2021 bull / 2022 crash). Confirm short-side feasibility/borrow for high-vol alts; if alts can't be shorted, recast as long-low-vol vs short-BTC-index and re-flag the construction.
proposal.crowding_risk: low — crypto factor investing is nascent; the short-lottery-alt leg is exactly where arbitrage/borrow constraints are tightest and retail lottery demand is most extreme, so the premium is least likely to be competed away.
proposal.generalization_plan: Mechanism is universal but the THESIS is that the tradable premium is corner-specific: it FAILED in crowded liquid US equities (BAB cross-market fail) and should survive in crypto precisely because of limits-to-arbitrage + extreme retail lottery demand. Guard against a single-lucky-universe artifact by testing within crypto across two UNTOUCHED sub-universes (large-cap top-20 vs mid-cap rank 20–60) — a real premium appears in both, ideally stronger in the harder-to-arbitrage mid tier; if it ONLY appears in the most-illiquid alt tier at unrealistic costs it is a cost mirage (BAB-small-cap lesson). Then forward-validate live to confirm out-of-sample persistence.

verdict.title: Low-risk / lottery cross-sectional equity factor (small-cap)

title: Crypto short-term reversal as a liquidity-provision risk premium (market-neutral, majors-only to control survivorship)

proposal.title: Crypto short-term reversal as a liquidity-provision risk premium (market-neutral, majors-only to control survivorship)
proposal.premium: Liquidity provision / short-term reversal (compensation for supplying immediacy to overreacting retail flow — a wiki-listed survivor: 'getting paid to provide liquidity'). NOT a forecast edge.
proposal.market: Crypto perpetual/spot — the least-arbitraged liquid corner
proposal.data_source: Binance/Bybit public API (daily close + dollar-volume panel for liquid coins; funding rates for cost modelling); validated trend leg via trend_returns()
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED/FREE (catalog: Binance/Bybit spot/perp/funding via public API; Boreas trend_returns ready)
proposal.signal_approach: Build a point-in-time universe of the top-30 USDT perps by trailing-30d dollar volume, reconstituted monthly and INCLUDING later-delisted symbols (survivorship control). PRIMARY (pre-registered, frozen): each week compute the trailing 7-calendar-day total return per coin, cross-sectionally demean, go LONG the bottom-tercile (biggest short-term losers) and SHORT the top-tercile (biggest winners), equal-weight within legs, scaled to dollar-neutral AND beta-neutral to BTC (regress and hedge the BTC beta) so the book isolates liquidity-provision, not crypto beta. Hold exactly 7 days, rebalance weekly. Apply realistic round-trip crypto costs (10bps/side taker) plus carried funding; no skip-day, no parameter grid, no win/lose tuning. Test STANDALONE first; only if it passes do we add the validated 21-market trend as a SMALL tail overlay (~25% vol weight, NOT 50/50) since liquidity provision is short-gamma and gets run over in liquidation cascades — exactly the inflationary/stress tail trend covers.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: reversal-trend-book FAILED but that was in DM FUTURES (efficient markets where reversal is arbitraged dead); crypto is a structurally different, retail-dominated, less-arbitraged corner. crypto-native-tsmom-standalone FAILED on the OPPOSITE signal (momentum). Funding-carry is a different cash-and-carry mechanism (forward-pending, not re-opened). No equity factor (closed substrate) is touched.
proposal.pairs_with: Validated 21-market trend crisis-alpha hedge, sized as a SMALL ~25% tail overlay (per the 2026-06-08 reflexive-50/50 anti-pattern) — reversal/liquidity-provision is short-gamma in crashes; trend is its complementary opposite tail.
proposal.gate0_data_check: Confirm we can pull a SURVIVORSHIP-CLEAN (delisted-included) weekly close + dollar-volume panel for ≥30 Binance/Bybit perps back to ~2019-2020, plus a delisting-events list and historical funding. CRITICAL: if only currently-listed coins are obtainable, the long-loser leg is catastrophically survivorship-biased (dead coins were the biggest losers) → drop prior to LOW and do not build. Verify ≥15 coins of cross-sectional breadth in the earliest years; verify taker-fee + funding assumptions are realistic.
proposal.crowding_risk: low — equity short-term reversal is crowded/decayed, but crypto short-term reversal is far less arbitraged: retail-dominated overreaction, high frictions and capital constraints deter institutional liquidity providers. A genuinely under-exploited implementation of a known mechanism in a less-efficient corner.
proposal.generalization_plan: Economic reason it is universe-specific: liquidity-provision/reversal is a UNIVERSAL mechanism but the PREMIUM only survives where limits-to-arbitrage are high — it is empirically DEAD in efficient markets (equity + DM-futures reversal both failed), and alive only in the retail-driven crypto corner. So I do not require broad cross-asset generalisation; instead the real-edge signature is: (1) the premium STRENGTHENS in smaller/mid-cap alt coins (more retail overreaction, less arbitrage) and WEAKENS in BTC/ETH (more efficient) — a monotone limits-to-arbitrage gradient that a fluke would not produce; (2) it replicates cross-VENUE on Bybit (independent order flow); (3) it survives the write-once holdout at realistic costs/funding and an MCPT permutation null (mandatory — to prove the reversal structure, not residual crypto beta, is the source); then forward-validation on live funding/fees confirms before any capital.

title: Hedging-Pressure (Commercial-Positioning) Risk Premium in Cross-Asset Futures — bearing the insurance hedgers pay to lay off price risk

proposal.title: Hedging-Pressure (Commercial-Positioning) Risk Premium in Cross-Asset Futures — bearing the insurance hedgers pay to lay off price risk
proposal.premium: Hedging pressure / normal-backwardation insurance premium (positioning-based, distinct from price-carry and trend) — speculators are paid to absorb the directional risk that commercial hedgers want to offload
proposal.market: Liquid commodity + financial futures (energies, metals, grains/softs, FX, rates, equity-index) — capacity-rich, retail-reachable via micro/mini contracts
proposal.data_source: Futures continuous prices via yf_panel (owned); CFTC Commitments-of-Traders commercial vs non-commercial net positions via the free CFTC public Socrata API (publicreporting.cftc.gov, no key)
proposal.free_or_owned: Prices OWNED (yfinance). COT is FREE & public ($0, no key) but NOT yet in the DATA_CATALOG → Gate-0 must verify before build (hence prior=low).
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN: For each futures market, compute the Hedging-Pressure index HP = (CommercialLong − CommercialShort)/(CommercialLong + CommercialShort) from weekly COT, then take its 3-year rolling percentile rank. Economic prior (Keynesian normal backwardation): when commercials are heavily net-SHORT (producers hedging output), speculators must go net-LONG and earn the insurance premium → go LONG that market; when commercials are net-LONG, go SHORT. PRIMARY = cross-sectional long-short: each week, vol-scale every market to equal risk, go long the top-tercile most speculator-favorable HP (commercials most short) and short the bottom tercile, equal risk-weighted, weekly rebalance. Strictly respect the COT reporting lag (positions are as-of Tuesday, released Friday) — trade only on/after the Friday release close to avoid look-ahead. Test STANDALONE first; only IF it shows positive net-of-cost expectancy, add the validated 21-market trend leg as a SMALL tail overlay (≤20–25%), never a reflexive 50/50 (per the 2026-06-08 dilution anti-pattern).
proposal.why_not_duplicate: All prior futures premia tested were PRICE-derived: boreas-carry-fxbond and commod-term-carry use the futures CURVE (term-structure carry); boreas-tsmom uses price MOMENTUM; reversal-trend-book uses short-horizon price reversal. This signal is POSITIONING-derived (who is on which side of the contract), a foundational and mechanistically distinct risk premium (the insurance hedgers pay speculators) never tested in the registry. It is not DM price-carry (closed) and not trend.
proposal.pairs_with: The validated 21-market crisis-alpha TREND hedge — but as a SMALL tail overlay (≤25%), added ONLY if it cuts drawdown without diluting standalone Sharpe. Hedging-pressure is pro-cyclical/carry-like (earns in calm, hurt in disorderly stress) → opposite tail to trend, the canonical complementary pairing.
proposal.gate0_data_check: (1) Confirm CFTC Socrata API returns legacy+disaggregated COT free with no key and sufficient history (legacy 1986+, disaggregated 2009+); (2) build a clean map from COT market/contract codes to yfinance continuous-futures tickers for ≥18 markets across ≥4 sectors; (3) verify the Tuesday-as-of / Friday-release lag handling produces NO look-ahead; (4) confirm weekly COT → daily price alignment and that delisted/rolled contracts don't corrupt the continuous series.
proposal.crowding_risk: medium — the academic hedging-pressure factor is published (Basu-Miffre, Gorton-Hayashi-Rouwenhorst, de Roon-Nijman-Veld), but the retail COT-positioning IMPLEMENTATION is far less arbitraged than equity value/momentum/BAB; this is a 'look where smart money isn't' positioning corner, not a crowded equity factor zoo.
proposal.generalization_plan: Broad/universal mechanism (hedgers exist in every futures market). Pre-register the full cross-sector book, then CONFIRM the premium appears in UNTOUCHED sector sub-baskets run independently — energies, metals, grains/softs, FX, rates, equity-index. A genuine hedging-pressure premium shows broad (even if weak) positive OOS across multiple sectors; an overfit one shows ONE lucky sector + negatives elsewhere (cf. the BAB single-universe-pass / cross-market-fail lesson). Require this cross-sector breadth BEFORE any forward track or trend overlay.

title: Earnings-Announcement Risk Premium (long predicted-announcers / short non-announcers, beta- & sector-neutral, mid-cap)

proposal.title: Earnings-Announcement Risk Premium (long predicted-announcers / short non-announcers, beta- & sector-neutral, mid-cap)
proposal.premium: Information-event / attention risk premium (Frazzini-Lamont): stocks earn abnormally high returns in the calendar month of their scheduled earnings announcement as compensation for bearing concentrated information-event risk (and from announcement-period attention/buying pressure). This is a distinct premium family from value/momentum/quality/accruals/BAB/issuance — it is paid for *bearing scheduled-event risk*, not for a cross-sectional fundamental or technical signal.
proposal.market: US equities, survivorship-clean mid-cap universe (where attention is constrained and the premium is least arbitraged); generalization-tested into small/large tiers.
proposal.data_source: Sharadar SEP via sep_panel (survivorship-clean adj prices + volume, delisted incl.) for returns; Sharadar SF1 via sf1(dimension='ARQ', use `datekey`) for point-in-time historical earnings-filing dates; us_universe(marketcap='Mid', include_delisted=True) for the universe.
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED/FREE — entirely on the Sharadar stack, $0, no Gate-0 data-gating.
proposal.signal_approach: Monthly rebalance. For each month t, PREDICT which names will announce in month t using ONLY their prior announcement cadence (next-expected datekey ≈ last observed SF1 datekey + ~91 days), strictly lagged so no upcoming actual date is ever used (no look-ahead). Within the survivorship-clean mid-cap universe (liquidity floor via trailing-60d SEP dollar-ADV), go LONG the predicted-announcer cohort and SHORT a matched non-announcer cohort drawn from the SAME universe, made beta-neutral (ex-ante 60d beta to the universe) and sector-neutral (us_universe sector tags), inverse-vol weighted within legs. Hold one month, rotate. Returns from sep_panel closeadj. Stress realistic mid-cap costs (15-30bps round-trip) and report holdout net-of-cost. The PRIMARY is this single pre-registered construction; no grid cherry-picking.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: It is NOT a value/momentum/quality/technical factor (those are the CLOSED 'standalone equity factor' set) — it is an event/risk-premium of a different family, never tested. It is explicitly NOT PEAD/SUE (pead_sue_midcap_v1, hephaestus-pead-pivot): PEAD conditions on the *surprise sign* and trades the post-announcement DRIFT; this is agnostic to the surprise and harvests the *announcement-month* premium itself. The beta- & sector-neutral long-short construction is designed to PASS the new stage-1 beta-confound rail (isolates announcement-cohort selection-alpha, not equity beta) — addressing the long-only-beta failure mode that killed value×mom.
proposal.pairs_with: Pro-cyclical (attention/buying pressure earned in calm/up regimes, prone to invert in stress) → complementary OPPOSITE-tail leg to the validated 21-market trend crisis-alpha hedge, and to the crypto funding-carry leg in an eventual multi-premium book. But per the 2026-06-08 anti-pattern, test STANDALONE first; add trend ONLY as a sized tail-overlay if it cuts the drawdown WITHOUT diluting Sharpe — never reflexive 50/50.
proposal.gate0_data_check: (1) Confirm SF1 `datekey` gives clean point-in-time filing dates with a stable ~quarterly cadence per ticker back to ~2005 for mid-caps; (2) verify the predict-next-from-prior-cadence rule reconstructs the announcement month with no use of the actual upcoming date (audit a few names across a known split/restatement for look-ahead); (3) confirm each month has enough predicted-announcers for a diversified book (≥40-50 names/leg in heavy fiscal months); (4) confirm SEP volume supports a tradable dollar-ADV liquidity floor so the short leg is borrowable in spirit (mid-cap, not micro).
proposal.crowding_risk: medium — published (Frazzini-Lamont 2007; Barber et al.) and partially decayed in large-caps as it became known, BUT the predict-the-announcement-month implementation is operationally costly, high-attention, and capacity-capped, so it remains under-exploited in the mid-cap/attention-constrained corner; the beta-/sector-neutral form is also off the well-traded factor axes.
proposal.generalization_plan: Universal information-event mechanism → a true premium must show broad OOS positivity. Pre-register the cross-market battery on UNTOUCHED cap-tiers: small-cap (expected STRONGEST — most attention-constrained/least arbitraged, per the 2026-06-09 small/illiquid lesson) and large-cap (expected weakest/decayed). A real premium = broad positive (even if weak) across tiers with the small>mid>large monotonic gradient the theory predicts; a pass in mid-cap alone with negatives elsewhere = a non-generalising outlier → REJECT. Mandatory MCPT (permute announcement-cohort membership, re-run) to confirm the edge is announcement-selection structure, not residual beta/inverse-vol; and a long-only-inverse-vol benchmark gate. Holdout is the sole arbiter; realistic mid-cap costs stressed.

title: Amihud illiquidity risk premium in survivorship-clean US small/mid-cap equities (long illiquid / short liquid, size- & beta-neutral, realistic-cost gated)

proposal.title: Amihud illiquidity risk premium in survivorship-clean US small/mid-cap equities (long illiquid / short liquid, size- & beta-neutral, realistic-cost gated)
proposal.premium: illiquidity (liquidity risk premium — compensation for bearing/providing illiquidity; a limits-to-arbitrage risk premium, NOT a forecast)
proposal.market: US small + mid-cap equities (survivorship-clean Sharadar SEP, delisted included)
proposal.data_source: Sharadar SEP (closeadj + volume) for prices/dollar-volume + us_universe (marketcap buckets, sector tags, delisted included); Boreas trend_returns only as an optional later tail-overlay
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED/FREE — fully buildable on the owned Sharadar stack at $0, no Gate-0 risk
proposal.signal_approach: Universe: survivorship-clean US small+mid caps via us_universe(marketcap='Small'/'Mid', include_delisted=True), 2000-present, with a price/dollar-volume floor to drop untradeable sub-$1 names. Each month compute Amihud illiquidity per name = trailing-12-month mean of |daily return| / daily dollar-volume (volume×close) from sep_panel. To isolate illiquidity from the size premium, FIRST sort into size terciles (and optionally sector buckets via TICKERS tags), then WITHIN each bucket sort on Amihud and build a dollar-neutral, beta-neutral long-short: LONG the most-illiquid quintile, SHORT the most-liquid quintile, equal-weighted, beta-matched using trailing 60-day betas, monthly rebalance with hysteresis. Frozen PRIMARY config; no grid cherry-picking. Report NET of pre-registered realistic costs: 60bps round-trip on the illiquid (long) leg, 15bps on the liquid (short) leg. Test STANDALONE first; only if it PASSES holdout+MCPT do we test adding a SMALL (≤25%) trend tail-overlay to trim the liquidity-crisis drawdown.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: The illiquidity premium has only ever been tested here in CRYPTO (the Midas illiquidity+carry near-miss). Equity Amihud illiquidity is UNTESTED in this portfolio and is a distinct factor family from everything already nulled (value, momentum, quality, BAB/low-vol, net-share-issuance, accruals, PEAD/SUE). It is a liquidity RISK premium (you are paid to bear/provide liquidity), not a standalone prediction edge — and it is built long-short/beta-neutral specifically to dodge the long-only-beta confound that killed value×mom.
proposal.pairs_with: Boreas 21-market trend (crisis-alpha) — the illiquidity premium is pro-cyclical (it crashes in liquidity crunches: 2008, 2020), so trend is its natural opposite-tail hedge. But per the over-blend anti-pattern, test illiquidity STANDALONE first and only add trend as a small tail-overlay if it cuts the crisis drawdown WITHOUT diluting Sharpe.
proposal.gate0_data_check: (1) Confirm SEP `volume` is RAW unadjusted share volume (not split-adjusted) and is populated for small/mid names across 2000+ (spot-check 5 names incl. a delisted one); (2) verify dollar-volume = volume×close is sane (no zeros/NaN clustering); (3) confirm us_universe('Small'/'Mid', include_delisted=True) returns enough names per month (≥several hundred) to form quintiles within size terciles; (4) confirm delisted tickers ARE included (survivorship-clean) so the short-liquid/long-illiquid legs aren't biased; (5) confirm no DATA-GATED source is needed — all owned.
proposal.crowding_risk: medium — Amihud (2002) is published, but it is structurally under-arbitraged BY CONSTRUCTION: the illiquidity that creates the premium is the same illiquidity that blocks arbitrage (a limits-to-arbitrage premium that lives in small/illiquid names exactly per the 2026-06-09 lesson). Less crowded/decayed than BAB/value/momentum/size. The dominant risk here is COST-FRAGILITY, not crowding — which is why realistic costs are pre-registered as the hard gate, and why mid-cap (not micro-cap) is expected to be the deployable tier.
proposal.generalization_plan: Illiquidity is a UNIVERSAL mechanism but MONOTONE in liquidity, so generalization = monotonicity not uniformity: (a) the premium must be MONOTONICALLY increasing across liquidity-sorted quintiles (strongest in least-liquid, weakening — but NOT reversing — toward liquid); (b) it must appear across multiple SECTORS within small/mid (not one lucky sector); (c) it must survive in BOTH the small AND mid cap tiers at realistic costs (mid-cap, lower-cost, is the deployable version per the BAB lesson). Large-cap is EXPECTED to be ~zero by construction (liquid names have no illiquidity to be paid for) — large-cap absence is CONSISTENT with the mechanism, not a generalization failure. Mandatory MCPT vs a within-size permutation null + holdout at realistic costs are the arbiters; forward-validate the mid-cap deployable version.

verdict.title: Amihud Illiquidity Risk Premium (Survivorship-Clean Small-Cap, Long/Short)

title: Value × Momentum + trend-overlay — MID-cap TIER-NATIVE re-tune (multi-tier scale-out leg)

proposal.title: Value × Momentum + trend-overlay — MID-cap TIER-NATIVE re-tune (multi-tier scale-out leg)
proposal.premium: Value (point-in-time book-to-market) + Momentum (12-1) complementary equity factor premia, 50/50 composite with a broad-market trend overlay
proposal.market: US Mid-cap equities (Sharadar SEP/SF1, survivorship-clean incl. delisted)
proposal.data_source: Sharadar SEP closeadj + SF1 ARQ bvps (datekey-lagged); us_universe(marketcap='Mid')
proposal.free_or_owned: owned
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN construction (reproduce the validated small-cap trend-overlay book at this cap tier): Universe = US Domestic Common Stock at marketcap='Mid', survivorship-clean (include_delisted=True), built per-sector across the 11 GICS-style sectors via us_universe(sector=, marketcap='Mid', top_n~130) then bounded (~1.0-1.3k liquid names). Prices = sep_panel closeadj. Value = point-in-time book-to-market (sf1 ARQ 'bvps' forward-filled from its 'datekey' filing date / split-adj close — NO look-ahead). Momentum = 12-1 (px.shift(21)/px.shift(252)-1). Winsorise both at 5/95 pct then daily cross-sectional z-score. Composite = 50/50 blend (w_value=0.5, w_mom=0.5). Long-only TOP TERCILE (score >= 2/3 quantile), inverse-vol sized (vol_lb~63d). Weekly rebalance (last trading day of each ISO week) with a no-trade HYSTERESIS band (keep held names whose score >= 2/3 - band). Broad-market TREND overlay: equal-weight universe index, risk-OFF to cash when index < its trailing MA (trend_ma~200d), overlay lagged 1 day. All signals lagged 1 day; ~8bps turnover cost. CRITICAL — the grid (value_only, mom_only, no_trend, tight_band, and a trend_ma length variant) is searched ON THIS TIER so PBO/DSR are measured on the tier's OWN data; the goal is a default config that is ROBUST (low PBO) on its native tier, NOT a config lifted from another tier.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: 2026-06-09 CPCV-hardened generalization (forward/valmom_generalization_cpcv.jsonl) proved the V+M MECHANISM generalises across ALL cap tiers (OOS-positive, CPCV-median-positive, 100% positive paths) but a config tuned on Small/Mid does NOT transfer to Mid (PBO 0.71-0.97 out-of-tier; low only on own discovery tier). This is the Mid-cap-NATIVE book — grid searched on Mid so the default ranks robustly on its own tier — the missing leg of a true multi-tier V+M portfolio (Small trend_overlay PBO 0.078 already validated).
proposal.pairs_with: the validated Small-cap trend_overlay book + the other tier-native books -> 3-tier V+M portfolio
proposal.gate0_data_check: us_universe(marketcap='Mid', include_delisted=True) returns ~1k names; sf1 'bvps' coverage adequate on this tier
proposal.crowding_risk: high — V+M is published/crowded (esp. large-cap); the real test is whether a TIER-NATIVE construction still clears CPCV/PBO/DSR on this tier's OWN OOS
proposal.generalization_plan: broad: the multi-tier portfolio itself is the generalization evidence — confirm the mechanism holds with low own-tier PBO here, then combine with the Large, Small books

verdict.title: Value x Momentum + trend-overlay — MID-cap TIER-NATIVE re-tune

title: Value × Momentum complementary combination in survivorship-clean US SMALL/MID caps — LONG-ONLY integrated-composite, cost-hardened, TREND REGIME-OVERLAY crisis-defended, NOW SECTOR-NEUTRALIZED (cross-sectional-concentration-defended deployable variant)

proposal.title: Value × Momentum complementary combination in survivorship-clean US SMALL/MID caps — LONG-ONLY integrated-composite, cost-hardened, TREND REGIME-OVERLAY crisis-defended, NOW SECTOR-NEUTRALIZED (cross-sectional-concentration-defended deployable variant)
proposal.premium: Same two complementary equity premia captured at the name level via an integrated composite rank (value = pro-cyclical mean-reversion + cross-sectional momentum = trend-following, ~−0.4/−0.5 correlated, Asness/Moskowitz/Pedersen), harvested LONG-ONLY so diversification-as-edge deploys without short borrow, wrapped in the firm's already-VALIDATED broad-market trend crisis-alpha (BOREAS) as a single mechanical net-exposure scaler. The NEW mutation addresses the SECOND structural fragility the trend overlay does not touch: a long-only value tilt mechanically OVERWEIGHTS whatever sector is statistically cheap (historically Financials/Energy/cyclicals), and momentum overweights whatever sector is hot — so the un-neutralized book carries a large UNINTENDED concentrated SECTOR bet that is itself a primary driver of crisis drawdown (long-only value was structurally long Financials straight INTO 2008). Computing value_z and momentum_z WITHIN sector (sector-neutral cross-sectional ranks) converts the book from a concentrated cyclical bet into a sector-balanced, name-selection book — extending diversification-as-edge from across-SIGNALS to also across-SECTORS (patterns #1/#2). It is risk management / orthogonalization, not a new forecast (pattern #3), uses only owned data, and complements the overlay: the trend overlay defends the market-wide timing tail, sector-neutralization defends the cross-sectional concentration tail.
proposal.market: US equities, SMALL + MID cap (survivorship-clean Sharadar), unlevered long-only cash equity, with NET equity exposure scaled 0–100% by the trend overlay (de-risked capital parked in cash / T-bill proxy at ~risk-free, never shorted, never levered), and cross-sectional book composition SECTOR-NEUTRALIZED within the Sharadar sector taxonomy.
proposal.data_source: Sharadar SEP (sep_panel — adjusted prices for momentum + price denominator + volume for ADV floor + the broad-market reference series for the trend overlay), Sharadar SF1 (sf1 — bvps ARQ point-in-time via datekey for book-to-market), Sharadar TICKERS (us_universe marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True; AND the sector/industry field used for the NEW sector-neutral z-scoring and per-sector weight caps — already OWNED, no new spend). Trend-overlay reference = the equal-weight small/mid universe price series (reconstructed survivorship-clean from SEP) and, as a robustness cross-check, a cap-index proxy from SEP — both OWNED.
proposal.free_or_owned: OWNED / FREE ($0, no Gate-0 spend risk)
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN from parent: Universe = survivorship-clean US names via us_universe(marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True); tradability floor = 60-day median dollar-volume > $1M; monthly signal; VALUE = bvps(ARQ, lagged to datekey)/price; MOMENTUM = 12-1 total return (skip last month). TREND REGIME OVERLAY UNCHANGED: slow broad-market trend state = reference price vs trailing 10-month MA (Faber/absolute-momentum, ONE frozen threshold); ABOVE → 100% net exposure, BELOW → frozen 50% (graded/binary-to-cash only as a small robustness grid); de-risked capital earns the cash/T-bill proxy; flips counted 1998–2024; overlay turnover included in net figures. Cost-hardening net Sharpe at 25/50/75 bps round-trip, verdict reads off 50 bps. NEW — SECTOR-NEUTRALIZATION (the mutation), kept deliberately simple to avoid the over-fit/over-blend anti-pattern: (a) assign each name its Sharadar sector; (b) compute value_z and momentum_z as cross-sectional z-scores WITHIN sector (demean & scale each factor by its own-sector distribution that month) rather than over the whole universe — ONE frozen choice (sector level, not the finer industry level, to keep enough names per bucket); (c) integrated composite_z = 0.5·value_z_sectorneutral + 0.5·momentum_z_sectorneutral per name/month; (d) deployable book = LONG-ONLY top-tercile of composite_z, equal-weight, BUT with a frozen per-SECTOR weight cap (each sector capped near its universe weight, e.g. ±5pp band) and a small per-NAME cap (e.g. 3%) for deployment sanity; (e) thin-sector guard: any sector with <15 tradable names that month falls back to full-universe z for those names (pre-registered), so neutralization never operates on a non-statistical bucket. Turnover hardening unchanged: no-trade band (enter ≤30th pct, exit >50th pct) + optional 3-month signal smoothing, target <60%/yr one-way, sector-neutral form's incremental turnover measured and INCLUDED in the 50 bps net figures. DIAGNOSTIC (not the verdict): report the book (i) raw, (ii) +trend overlay, (iii) +trend overlay +sector-neutral, to isolate EACH defense's marginal contribution and confirm sector-neutralization cuts the 2008-type concentrated-cyclical drawdown at little-to-no Sharpe cost. Also still report each leg standalone, leg-to-leg correlation, and the in-sample L/S quintile combo to confirm the premium structurally exists. Pre-registered success = the overlaid sector-neutral long-only composite beats the equal-weight small/mid benchmark on Sharpe AND MAR net of 50 bps, MATERIALLY cuts the 2008/2020/2022 drawdown vs BOTH the un-overlaid and the overlay-only-but-sector-concentrated books, the negative value-momentum correlation still visibly stabilizes the tilt, and the realized sector exposures are demonstrably balanced (no single sector >cap); the write-once HOLDOUT is the SOLE arbiter.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: atlas-equity-factors tested value AND momentum STANDALONE in LARGE-liquid caps → FAIL (closed). This remains the textbook COMPLEMENTARY pair in a new small/mid survivorship-clean substrate, and now adds a second, independent diversification axis the parent and all closed work lack: SECTOR-neutral cross-sectional ranking that removes the hidden concentrated cyclical bet which (alongside crisis beta) actually produced the long-only value 50% 2008 drawdown. It is distinct from the parent (which fixed only the market-wide timing tail via the trend overlay) and is NOT trend-as-standalone (BOREAS proved that worthless). Sector-neutralization is a validated orthogonalization (industry-adjusted value/momentum, Asness et al. — both improve net of the sector bet), used here as risk management, not a new signal, so it cannot be a re-test of any closed standalone-factor result.
proposal.pairs_with: Internally self-hedging (value pro-cyclical; momentum crashes on post-crash reversals — opposite tails), externally crisis-defended by the trend overlay, and now cross-sectionally de-concentrated by sector-neutralization (removes the unintended sector tail). Long-only + overlay + sector-neutral form is additive to the carry+trend book with no short-borrow conflict; the overlay shares the SAME trend crisis-alpha mechanism already in the book. Optional later: a low-beta/defensive z as a SMALL additional composite_z term (NOT a reflexive 50/50) only if it further cuts combined drawdown without diluting Sharpe — now safer to add because sector-neutralization already absorbs much of the latent defensive/cyclical sector spread.
proposal.gate0_data_check: 1) us_universe(marketcap='Small') and 'Mid' each return enough survivorship-clean names (>500 small, >300 mid), delisted included. 2) SF1 bvps point-in-time coverage for small/mid names, datekey (not calendardate) as as-of. 3) SEP price+volume spans the same names with ≥12-month momentum lookback plus 2008/2020/2022 stress. 4) $1M ADV floor leaves ≥100+ names so the top-tercile book is genuinely diversified (deployment-sanity). 5) Verify (a) the equal-weight small/mid benchmark is reconstructable survivorship-clean and (b) the no-trade band materially cuts turnover in-sample without destroying signal. 6) Trend overlay: confirm broad-market reference series reconstructable over the full 10-month-MA window from 1998+, a cash/T-bill proxy is available (else default 0% conservatively), and COUNT regime flips 1998–2024 to confirm the overlay is slow. 7) NEW for sector-neutralization: (a) confirm Sharadar TICKERS sector field has coverage for the small/mid survivorship-clean universe across 1998+ (including delisted names) so sector membership is point-in-time usable, not just current; (b) verify the number of TRADABLE names per sector per month — confirm most months have ≥15 names in the major sectors so within-sector z-scores are statistical, and quantify how often the thin-sector fallback fires; (c) confirm the top-tercile sector-capped book still holds ≥100 names diversified across ≥6 sectors (deployment-sanity), i.e. neutralization does not collapse the book; (d) measure the incremental one-way turnover from sector caps + sector-neutral ranks BEFORE committing the holdout, to confirm it stays inside the <60%/yr budget at 50 bps.
proposal.crowding_risk: Value/momentum are the two most-published factors → crowded in large-liquid LEVERED-L/S quant books (the Aug-2007 quake was a levered-L/S deleveraging event). This variant sidesteps that channel: small/mid + tradability-floored is less arbitraged, and an UNLEVERED LONG-ONLY tilt has no short-crowding/forced-cover exposure with deep $-capacity. The trend overlay is a mechanical risk-management layer (uncrowdable). Sector-neutralization is likewise a mechanical orthogonalization with no added capacity drag and, if anything, REDUCES crowding-correlated drawdown by not piling into the same cheap sector every other long-only value book also overweights. Residual risk = benchmark-relative tilt compression (monitored via the live tercile spread vs history), overlay whipsaw in choppy markets (bounded by the slow MA + monthly cadence, measured by the with/without-overlay diagnostic), and a mild momentum-signal dilution from within-sector ranking (explicitly measured by the sector-neutral vs un-neutral diagnostic — kept only if Sharpe cost is small relative to the drawdown gain).
proposal.scope: Research/backtest over full SEP history (1998+) incl. 2008/2020/2022 stress; deployable form is unlevered long-only cash equity, sector-balanced, with net exposure scaled 0–100% by the trend overlay, retail-feasible from small AUM and scalable (long-only small/mid + cash overlay + sector caps has deep capacity).
proposal.generalization_plan: The banded, integrated-composite, SECTOR-NEUTRAL long-only template PLUS a slow trend exposure-overlay generalizes 'diversification-as-edge' across THREE axes simultaneously — across complementary signals, across sectors, and across market regimes — WITHOUT a short book, and gives any long-only premium book a reusable crisis-defense + concentration-defense stack. Future complementary legs (defensive/low-beta) slot in as composite_z terms (sector-neutralized too); the overlay and sector-neutralization slot onto any long-only sleeve. Robustness battery before the holdout: (a) small vs mid buckets separately, (b) net Sharpe across 25/50/75 bps, (c) no-trade band width sensitivity, (d) 50/50 vs alternative leg weights, (e) overlay sensitivity: 10-month MA vs 200-day MA vs 12-month absolute-momentum, de-risk-to-50% vs to-cash, (f) NEW — sector-neutralization sensitivity: sector-level vs full-universe (un-neutral) z, sector-cap width (no cap / ±5pp / strict equal-sector), and sector vs finer-industry neutralization (expecting industry to be too thin in small/mid — pre-registered hypothesis), reported as a SMALL frozen grid to prove the concentration-driven drawdown cut is structural (not a single-parameter artifact). Write-once holdout is the sole arbiter; success requires the overlay AND sector-neutralization to each cut crisis drawdown at little-to-no Sharpe cost, mirroring the validated carry+trend result.
proposal.mutation_note: MUTATION = SECTOR-NEUTRALIZE the cross-sectional composite. Compute value_z and momentum_z WITHIN Sharadar sector (with a pre-registered thin-sector fallback to full-universe z) and add frozen per-sector (±~5pp) and per-name (~3%) weight caps, converting the long-only book from a hidden concentrated cyclical bet into a sector-balanced name-selection book. Keeps everything that worked (integrated composite, long-only deployability, turnover band, cost-hardening, trend overlay) and fixes the SECOND structural fragility the trend overlay does not address — the unintended concentrated sector tail (long-only value was structurally long Financials into 2008) — using a validated orthogonalization (risk management, not a new forecast) on already-owned sector data, isolated by a three-way raw / +overlay / +overlay+sector-neutral diagnostic.

title: Crypto liquidity-provision premium (weekly cross-sectional reversal) — a CHOP-regime complement to the validated trend & carry legs, tested STANDALONE in the least-arbitraged corner

proposal.title: Crypto liquidity-provision premium (weekly cross-sectional reversal) — a CHOP-regime complement to the validated trend & carry legs, tested STANDALONE in the least-arbitraged corner
proposal.premium: Liquidity provision / short-horizon mean-reversion — compensation for absorbing uninformed/forced demand shocks (Nagel 2012 'Evaporating Liquidity'; Campbell-Grossman-Wang). Framed as a RISK PREMIUM (inventory + adverse-selection risk), NOT a price forecast: you get paid to take the other side of impatient retail flow.
proposal.market: Crypto perpetual futures (USDT-margined), market-neutral / beta-neutral to BTC.
proposal.data_source: Binance/Bybit public API (perp daily closes + funding, the same access already powering carry_returns()); plus Sharadar SEP + us_universe(marketcap=Micro/Small/Large) for the owned-equity liquidity-gradient generalization legs.
proposal.free_or_owned: Owned/free. Crypto via the free public API (carry_returns() proves access; a thin price-panel adapter is the only build). Equity gradient legs are fully owned, survivorship-clean.
proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN PRIMARY: weekly formation/hold. Universe = top ~60 USDT-perps by trailing-30d ADV, INCLUDING delisted/removed perps (survivorship-handled). Signal = prior-week return, cross-sectionally demeaned by removing the BTC/market component (idiosyncratic reversal). Go LONG the bottom quintile (worst idiosyncratic performers / liquidity-demand sellers) and SHORT the top quintile (best / liquidity-demand buyers); inverse-vol weight within each leg; enforce dollar-neutral AND beta-to-BTC≈0; rebalance weekly; size to vol-match the trend leg. Costs: 10bps taker round-trip + funding accrual on shorts, STRESS-tested to 30bps (reversal is high-turnover → cost is the kill-switch per the small-cap cost-fragility lesson). Holdout = final ~30% of history, write-once.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: Reversal was tested only in FUTURES, reflexively PAIRED with trend (reversal-trend-book → FAIL) — that fail is fully consistent with the mechanism being arbitraged-to-zero in liquid markets. This tests the SAME premium in the LEAST-arbitraged corner (crypto), STANDALONE first (not blended into trend), as a distinct CHOP/mean-reverting regime leg. It is the OPPOSITE sign of crypto-native-tsmom (momentum, FAIL) and a different mechanism from Midas (illiquidity-LEVEL relative-value pair, not return-reversal).
proposal.pairs_with: The validated 21-market trend (crisis-alpha, earns big moves / bleeds chop) and crypto funding-carry (pro-cyclical, earns calm / crashes in stress). Reversal earns in choppy, range-bound, mean-reverting tape — the regime where BOTH other legs are weak — making it the natural THIRD complementary leg toward a regime-complete multi-premium book. BUT validate standalone first; add only as a small overlay if it cuts the book's tail WITHOUT diluting Sharpe (per the 2026-06-08 anti-trend-over-blend lesson).
proposal.gate0_data_check: (1) Verify the public API returns daily klines for ≥50 liquid USDT-perps from inception (~2019-2020) AND can recover DELISTED/removed perps — many alts delist, so survivorship is the primary data risk; if unrecoverable, flag and prior→low. proposal.title: Value × Momentum complementary combination in survivorship-clean US SMALL/MID caps — LONG-ONLY integrated-composite, cost-hardened, TREND REGIME-OVERLAY + NEW frozen PROFITABILITY JUNK-SCREEN (value-trap & single-name-blowup-defended deployable variant)

The MUTATION adds a third, documented, DEFENSIVE premium used as a SCREEN rather than a co-equal signal: PROFITABILITY/QUALITY (Novy-Marx gross profitability; Asness-Frazzini-Pedersen QMJ — a decades-OOS, defensive, flight-to-quality premium). Rationale for the fix: the parent's binding fragility was MARKET-WIDE crisis drawdown, which the trend overlay handles; but a LONG-ONLY SMALL/MID VALUE tilt over a survivorship-clean (delisted-included) universe is structurally LOADED with distressed 'value traps' — cheap-because-dying names that blow up IDIOSYNCRATICALLY (single-name, not market-wide). That left-tail is INVISIBLE to a broad-market MA overlay, so it is the residual deploy fragility the overlay structurally cannot catch. A profitability junk-screen removes that tail at source.

proposal.data_source: Sharadar SEP (sep_panel — adjusted prices for momentum + price denominator + volume for ADV floor + the broad-market reference series for the trend overlay), Sharadar SF1 (sf1 — bvps for book-to-market AND NEW profitability inputs: gross profit / revenue / COGS / total assets, all ARQ point-in-time via datekey, never calendardate), Sharadar TICKERS (us_universe marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True). proposal.signal_approach: FROZEN from parent (unchanged): Universe = survivorship-clean US names via us_universe(marketcap in {Small,Mid}, include_delisted=True); tradability floor = 60-day median dollar-volume > $1M; monthly signal; VALUE = bvps(ARQ, lagged to datekey)/price, cross-sectional z; MOMENTUM = 12-1 total return (skip last month), cross-sectional z; INTEGRATED composite_z = 0.5·value_z + 0.5·momentum_z; deployable book = LONG-ONLY top-tercile of composite_z, equal-weight; turnover hardening via no-trade band (enter ≤30th pct, exit >50th pct) + optional 3-month smoothing, target <60%/yr; cost-hardening net Sharpe at 25/50/75 bps round-trip, verdict reads off the 50 bps case; TREND OVERLAY = reference price vs trailing 10-month MA (Faber/abs-momentum), ABOVE → 100% net, BELOW → frozen 50%, de-risked cash earns T-bill proxy; overlay flips counted to confirm slowness, its cost included in net figures. NEW — PROFITABILITY JUNK-SCREEN (the mutation), kept deliberately as a SCREEN not a co-equal factor to avoid the over-blend anti-pattern: (a) compute one frozen profitability metric = gross profitability = (revenue − COGS) / total assets, ARQ point-in-time via datekey, cross-sectional rank per month; (b) DROP the bottom quality quintile from the ELIGIBLE universe BEFORE the value-momentum composite ranking (removes distressed value traps so they can never enter the long-only top-tercile); (c) the validated 0.5/0.5 value-momentum composite, bands, overlay, and tercile construction are otherwise UNCHANGED. ROBUSTNESS-ONLY (not the deploy form): also report quality as a SMALL frozen composite term (0.4·value_z + 0.4·momentum_z + 0.2·quality_z) to confirm the effect is structural, not a screen artifact. DIAGNOSTIC (not the verdict): report the book WITH and WITHOUT the screen to isolate the value-trap-defense contribution — expect REDUCED single-name blowup contribution and a thinner idiosyncratic left tail at little-to-no Sharpe cost; also still report each leg standalone, leg-to-leg correlation, the in-sample L/S quintile combo, and the with/without-overlay diagnostic from the parent. Pre-registered success = screened, overlaid long-only composite beats the equal-weight small/mid benchmark on Sharpe AND MAR net of 50 bps, MATERIALLY cuts both market-wide (2008/2020/2022, via overlay) AND idiosyncratic/single-name (via screen) drawdown vs the unscreened+un-overlaid book, with the negative value-momentum correlation still visibly stabilizing the tilt; the write-once HOLDOUT is the SOLE arbiter.
proposal.why_not_duplicate: atlas-equity-factors tested value AND momentum (and profitability) STANDALONE in LARGE-liquid caps → FAIL (edge-bound, closed). This is the COMPLEMENTARY pair (negative correlation = diversification edge, pattern #1/#2) in a new small/mid survivorship-clean substrate, long-only + cost-hardened + crisis-overlaid (the parent). The MUTATION does NOT re-test quality as a standalone factor (already closed) — it uses profitability ONLY as a DEFENSIVE JUNK-SCREEN to remove distressed value traps, the documented QMJ mechanism (cheap-junk is the part of value that crashes). This is the classic value+quality+momentum trio (Asness et al.) but expressed as a long-only small/mid screen under a trend overlay — a deploy-hardening layer, not a new premium. It fixes a fragility (idiosyncratic single-name blowups in survivorship-clean small/mid value) that the parent's market-wide overlay structurally CANNOT catch, using a mechanism with decades of OOS evidence — not a new untested idea.
proposal.pairs_with: Internally self-hedging (value pro-cyclical; momentum crashes on reversals — opposite tails) AND now doubly crisis-defended: the trend overlay handles MARKET-WIDE drawdown, the profitability screen handles SINGLE-NAME/idiosyncratic blowups — orthogonal protections, no overlap. Quality is a defensive premium (flight-to-quality), opposite-tail to value, so the screen reinforces rather than duplicates the existing risk layers. Long-only form remains additive to the carry+trend book with no short-borrow conflict.
proposal.gate0_data_check: 1) us_universe Small (>500) and Mid (>300) survivorship-clean, delisted included. 2) SF1 bvps AND profitability inputs (revenue, COGS, total assets) point-in-time coverage for small/mid via datekey — confirm gross profitability is computable for ≥~80% of names per month (and that screen-out for missing data is conservative, not look-ahead). 3) SEP price+volume spans the names with ≥12-month momentum lookback plus 2008/2020/2022 stress. 4) DEPLOYMENT-SANITY: after the $1M ADV floor AND dropping the bottom profitability quintile, ≥100+ names remain so the top-tercile book is still genuinely diversified. 5) Verify (a) the equal-weight small/mid benchmark is reconstructable survivorship-clean and (b) the no-trade band materially cuts turnover in-sample. 6) Overlay checks unchanged: reference series reconstructable over the full 10-month-MA window from 1998+, cash/T-bill proxy available (else default 0%), regime flips counted to confirm slowness. 7) NEW: confirm the junk-screen's added turnover is small (screen membership is slow-moving fundamentals) and that, in-sample, screened-out names had measurably higher delisting/blowup frequency — i.e. the screen is doing what it claims BEFORE committing the holdout.
proposal.crowding_risk: Value/momentum are the two most-published factors → crowded in large-liquid LEVERED-L/S books (Aug-2007 was a levered-L/S deleveraging event). This variant sidesteps that channel: small/mid + tradability-floored is less arbitraged, and an UNLEVERED LONG-ONLY tilt has no short-crowding/forced-cover exposure with deep $-capacity. The trend overlay and the profitability screen are mechanical risk-management layers (uncrowdable, negligible capacity drag — the screen only REMOVES names, raising average liquidity). Residual risks = benchmark-relative tilt compression (monitored via live tercile spread vs history), overlay whipsaw in choppy markets (bounded by slow MA + monthly cadence, measured by with/without-overlay diagnostic), and quality being itself somewhat published — mitigated by using it as a conservative bottom-quintile SCREEN rather than a tradable factor.
proposal.scope: Research/backtest over full SEP history (1998+) incl. 2008/2020/2022 stress; deployable form is unlevered long-only cash equity, profitability-screened universe, net exposure scaled 0–100% by the trend overlay — retail-feasible from small AUM and scalable (long-only small/mid + cash overlay has deep capacity; screen improves average liquidity).
proposal.generalization_plan: The banded integrated-composite long-only template + slow trend exposure-overlay + profitability junk-screen together generalize 'diversification-as-edge' to ANY complementary long-only premium book AND give it BOTH a market-wide crisis-defense (overlay) AND an idiosyncratic-blowup defense (junk-screen) — two reusable, mechanical, orthogonal hardening layers that any long-only sleeve can adopt. Robustness battery before the holdout: (a) small vs mid buckets separately; (b) net Sharpe across 25/50/75 bps; (c) no-trade band width sensitivity; (d) 50/50 vs alternative leg weights; (e) overlay sensitivity (10-month MA vs 200-day MA vs 12-month abs-mom; de-risk-to-50% vs to-cash); (f) NEW — screen sensitivity: bottom-quintile vs bottom-decile screen, quality metric = gross profitability vs ROA vs gross margin, and screen-form vs small-composite-term (0.2 weight) form, reported as a SMALL frozen grid to prove the value-trap defense is structural, not a single-parameter artifact; plus the with/without-screen diagnostic. Write-once holdout is the SOLE arbiter; success requires the screen to cut idiosyncratic left-tail/blowup drawdown at little-to-no Sharpe cost, mirroring the validated crisis-alpha pattern.
proposal.mutation_note: MUTATION = added a single, frozen PROFITABILITY (gross-profitability) JUNK-SCREEN that removes the lowest-quality quintile from the eligible universe BEFORE composite ranking — fixing the RESIDUAL deploy fragility (distressed 'value-trap' single-name/idiosyncratic blowups in a survivorship-clean long-only small/mid VALUE tilt) that the parent's broad-market trend overlay structurally cannot catch because it is market-wide, not name-level. Keeps everything that worked unchanged (0.5/0.5 value-momentum integrated composite, long-only top-tercile deployability, no-trade band, cost-hardening, the 10-month-MA trend exposure overlay) and imports a decades-OOS DEFENSIVE premium (QMJ/Novy-Marx) as a conservative screen — not a new co-equal factor — avoiding the over-blend anti-pattern.

(2) USD overlay: long broad USD (UUP, cross-check vs FRED DTWEXBGS), sized as a SMALL fixed tail-overlay = 20% of the book's risk budget (credit 80% / USD 20% by vol). This single weight is PRE-REGISTERED — no grid search over the blend (anti-pattern #2). (3) Realistic liquid-ETF costs (~3–5 bps). DISCIPLINE per the 2026-06-08 lesson: test the credit-carry STANDALONE first (must reproduce ~+0.27 search Sharpe, holdout-consistent), then the USD overlay PASSES only if it strictly LOWERS maxDD / left-tail across the GFC'08, COVID'20 and 2022 stress windows WHILE keeping combined Sharpe ≥ standalone Sharpe (cut the tail without diluting the premium).
proposal.why_not_duplicate: credit-carry-trend-book FAILED specifically because it blended TREND at 50/50, whose ~0-Sharpe (negative 2011-2020) decade dragged the real +0.27 credit premium to −0.017 (a documented FALSE-fail). This proposal swaps the hedge to USD (a different, non-diluting crisis asset that uniquely won in 2022 where bonds AND trend's chop hurt) and sizes it as a small 20% tail-overlay instead of a reflexive 50/50 — the exact corrective the 2026-06-08 anti-pattern prescribes. Different hedge + different sizing rule = a genuinely new hypothesis, not a re-run of a closed set.
proposal.pairs_with: Self-contained two-leg book. The USD micro-overlay mechanism is intended to generalise as a non-trend crisis hedge for ANY pro-cyclical premium — notably the live crypto funding-carry near-miss (currently only ever paired with trend) and IG credit (LQD).

proposal.crowding_risk: medium — the credit risk premium is institutionally known but persists as genuine risk compensation (not arbitraged away like predictions); the USD-as-small-overlay implementation is low-crowding/novel.
proposal.generalization_plan: Broad universal mechanism (pro-cyclical premium + reserve-currency flight-to-quality). Confirm in untouched settings: (a) the SAME 3/3 crisis-window stress test (GFC'08, COVID'20, 2022) that validated the trend hedge — USD must hedge the credit-spread blowout in EACH, especially 2022 where duration failed; (b) swap the pro-cyclical leg to IG credit (LQD) and to the existing crypto funding-carry leg — the USD overlay should cut the tail WITHOUT diluting Sharpe in each, proving the hedge mechanism (not a HY-specific fluke). proposal.crowding_risk: Value, momentum and quality are all heavily published → crowded in large-liquid LEVERED-L/S quant books (the Aug-2007 quake was a levered-L/S deleveraging event). Residual risk = benchmark-relative tilt compression (monitored via the live tercile spread vs history), overlay whipsaw in choppy markets (bounded by slow MA + monthly cadence, measured by the with/without-overlay diagnostic), and the small chance the quality screen removes a genuinely cheap-and-sound cohort (measured by the with/without-screen diagnostic — the screen is dropped if it dilutes Sharpe without cutting the left tail).

Write-once holdout is the sole arbiter; success requires the overlay to cut SYSTEMATIC crisis drawdown AND the screen to cut IDIOSYNCRATIC value-trap drawdown, both at little-to-no Sharpe cost.
proposal.mutation_note: MUTATION = added a single, pre-registered, slow-moving DEFENSIVE QUALITY SCREEN (gross-profits-to-assets, Novy-Marx, point-in-time via SF1 gp/assets at datekey) that EXCLUDES the worst-quality / distressed 'junk-value' names from the rankable universe BEFORE the value/momentum composite is formed. Keeps everything validated (0.5/0.5 integrated composite, long-only deployability, turnover band, cost-hardening, 10-mo-MA trend overlay) and fixes the parent's remaining binding fragility — a small/mid B/M tilt structurally over-buys cheap-because-dying distressed names whose idiosyncratic single-name blow-ups the broad-market trend overlay CANNOT hedge.
